\"torch.masked._ops.sum\",\n        \"torch.masked._ops.var\",\n        \"torch.meshgrid\",\n        \"torch.mps._get_default_mps_generator\",\n        \"torch.mps.current_allocated_memory\",\n        \"torch.mps.driver_allocated_memory\",\n        \"torch.mps.empty_cache\",\n        \"torch.mps.get_rng_state\",\n        \"torch.mps.manual_seed\",\n        \"torch.mps.profiler.profile\",\n        \"torch.mps.profiler.start\",\n        \"torch.mps.profiler.stop\",\n        \"torch.mps.seed\",\n        \"torch.mps.set_per_process_memory_fraction\",\n        \"torch.mps.set_rng_state\",\n        \"torch.mps.synchronize\",\n        \"torch.nested._internal.nested_tensor.buffer_from_jagged\",\n        \"torch.nested._internal.nested_tensor.get_tensor_symint\",\n        \"torch.nested._internal.nested_tensor.is_expandable_to\",\n        \"torch.nested._internal.nested_tensor.jagged_from_list\",\n        \"torch.nested._internal.nested_tensor.jagged_from_tensor_and_lengths\",\n        \"torch.nested._internal.nested_tensor.nested_view_from_values_offsets\",\n        \"torch.nested._internal.nested_tensor.nested_view_from_values_offsets_lengths\",\n        \"torch.nested.as_nested_tensor\",\n        \"torch.nested.narrow\",\n        \"torch.nested.nested_tensor\",\n        \"torch.nn._reduction.get_enum\",\n        \"torch.nn._reduction.legacy_get_enum\",\n        \"torch.nn._reduction.legacy_get_string\",\n        \"torch.nn.factory_kwargs\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_avg_pool2d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_avg_pool3d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_max_pool1d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_max_pool1d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_max_pool2d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_max_pool2d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_max_pool3d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.adaptive_max_pool3d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.affine_grid\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.alpha_dropout\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.assert_int_or_pair\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.batch_norm\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.binary_cross_entropy_with_logits\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.binary_cross_entropy\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.celu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.cosine_embedding_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.ctc_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.dropout\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.dropout1d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.dropout2d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.dropout3d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.elu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.embedding_bag\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.embedding\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.feature_alpha_dropout\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.fold\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.fractional_max_pool2d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.fractional_max_pool2d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.fractional_max_pool3d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.fractional_max_pool3d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.gaussian_nll_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.glu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.grid_sample\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.group_norm\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.gumbel_softmax\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.hardsigmoid\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.hardswish\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.hardtanh\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.hinge_embedding_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.huber_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.instance_norm\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.interpolate\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.kl_div\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.l1_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.layer_norm\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.leaky_relu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.local_response_norm\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.log_softmax\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.lp_pool1d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.lp_pool2d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.margin_ranking_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_pool1d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_pool1d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_pool2d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_pool2d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_pool3d_with_indices\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_pool3d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_unpool1d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_unpool2d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.max_unpool3d\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.mish\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.mse_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.multi_head_attention_forward\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.multi_margin_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.multilabel_margin_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.multilabel_soft_margin_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.nll_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.normalize\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.poisson_nll_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.relu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.relu6\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.rrelu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.selu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.sigmoid\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.silu\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.smooth_l1_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.soft_margin_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.softmax\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.softmin\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.softsign\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.tanh\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.tanhshrink\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.triplet_margin_loss\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.unfold\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.upsample_bilinear\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.upsample_nearest\",\n        \"torch.nn.functional.upsample\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad._pair\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad._single\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad._triple\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad.conv1d_input\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad.conv1d_weight\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad.conv2d_input\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad.conv2d_weight\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad.conv3d_input\",\n        \"torch.nn.grad.conv3d_weight\",\n        \"torch.nn.modules.activation._is_make_fx_tracing\",\n        \"torch.nn.modules.utils._list_with_default\",\n        \"torch.nn.modules.utils._ntuple\",\n        \"torch.nn.modules.utils._quadruple\",\n        \"torch.nn.modules.utils._reverse_repeat_tuple\",\n        \"torch.nn.modules.utils.consume_prefix_in_state_dict_if_present\",\n        \"torch.nn.parameter.is_lazy\",\n        \"torch.norm\",\n        \"torch.quantization.default_eval_fn\",\n        \"torch.random._seed_custom_device\",\n        \"torch.random.fork_rng\",\n        \"torch.random.initial_seed\",\n        \"torch.random.seed\",\n        \"torch.return_types.pytree_register_structseq\",\n        \"torch.set_default_dtype\",\n        \"torch.set_default_tensor_type\",\n        \"torch.set_deterministic_debug_mode\",\n        \"torch.set_float32_matmul_precision\",\n        \"torch.set_warn_always\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows._add_docstr\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows._window_function_checks\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.bartlett\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.blackman\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.cosine\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.exponential\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.gaussian\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.general_cosine\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.general_hamming\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.hamming\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.hann\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.kaiser\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.merge_dicts\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.nuttall\",\n        \"torch.signal.windows.windows.parse_kwargs\",\n        \"torch.sparse.semi_structured.to_sparse_semi_structured\",\n        \"torch.sparse.sum\",\n        \"torch.split\",\n        \"torch.stft\",\n        \"torch.sym_float\",\n        \"torch.sym_int\",\n        \"torch.sym_ite\",\n        \"torch.sym_max\",\n        \"torch.sym_min\",\n        \"torch.sym_not\",\n        \"torch.tensordot\",\n        \"torch.unique_consecutive\",\n        \"torch.use_deterministic_algorithms\",\n        \"torch.xpu._get_device\",\n        \"torch.xpu._get_generator\",\n        \"torch.xpu._get_rng_state_offset\",\n        \"torch.xpu._is_compiled\",\n        \"torch.xpu._lazy_call\",\n        \"torch.xpu._lazy_init\",\n        \"torch.xpu._set_rng_state_offset\",\n        \"torch.xpu._set_stream_by_id\",\n        \"torch.xpu._utils._get_device_index\",\n        \"torch.xpu.current_device\",\n        \"torch.xpu.current_stream\",\n        \"torch.xpu.device_count\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_arch_list\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_device_capability\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_device_name\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_device_properties\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_gencode_flags\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_stream_from_external\",\n        \"torch.xpu.init\",\n        \"torch.xpu.is_available\",\n        \"torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported\",\n        \"torch.xpu.is_initialized\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.empty_cache\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.max_memory_allocated\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.max_memory_reserved\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.mem_get_info\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_allocated\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_reserved\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_stats_as_nested_dict\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_stats\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.reset_accumulated_memory_stats\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.reset_peak_memory_stats\",\n        \"torch.xpu.random.initial_seed\",\n        \"torch.xpu.random.seed_all\",\n        \"torch.xpu.random.seed\",\n        \"torch.xpu.set_stream\",\n        \"torch.xpu.stream\",\n        \"torch.xpu.synchronize\",\n    ],\n    TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n)\n\n\ntorch_name_rule_map = [\n    manual_torch_name_rule_map,\n    torch_c_binding_in_graph_functions,\n    torch_non_c_binding_in_graph_functions,\n]\n\n\n\"\"\"\nGenerate the torch object - Dynamo tracing rule (the wrapping variable) map.\n\"\"\"\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_torch_obj_rule_map() -> dict[Any, type[\"VariableTracker\"]]:\n    d: dict[Any, type[VariableTracker]] = {}\n    for m in torch_name_rule_map:\n        for k, v in m.items():  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            if \".py#\" not in k:\n                obj = load_object(k)\n            else:\n                torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n                if torch_dir is None:\n                    continue\n                obj = torch_dir + k[len(\"torch/\") :]\n            if obj is not None:\n                if is_lru_cache_wrapped_function(obj):\n                    obj = obj.__wrapped__\n                if obj in d and d[obj] != v:\n                    raise AssertionError(\n                        f\"Duplicate torch object {obj} with different rules: {v}, {d[obj]}\"\n                    )\n                else:\n                    d[obj] = v\n    return d\n\n\ndef _load_obj_from_str(fully_qualified_name: str) -> Any:\n    module, obj_name = fully_qualified_name.rsplit(\".\", maxsplit=1)\n    return getattr(importlib.import_module(module), obj_name)\n\n\n\"\"\"\nLoad string represented torch objects.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_object(name: str) -> Any:\n    try:\n        x = name.split(\"#\")\n        if len(x) == 2:\n            obj = _load_obj_from_str(x[0])\n            val = getattr(obj, x[1])\n        else:\n            assert len(x) == 1, f\"Invalid obj name {name}\"\n            val = _load_obj_from_str(x[0])\n        val = unwrap_if_wrapper(val)\n    except (AttributeError, ImportError):\n        val = None\n    return val\n\n\n\"\"\"\nGet all torch.Tensor methods which are allowed to be in graph functions.\n\"\"\"\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_tensor_method() -> frozenset[Any]:\n    disallowed_tensor_methods = {\"__new__\", \"_make_wrapper_subclass\", \"_make_subclass\"}\n    s = set()\n    for name in dir(torch.Tensor):\n        method = getattr(torch.Tensor, name)\n        if (\n            isinstance(\n                method,\n                (\n                    types.MethodDescriptorType,\n                    types.WrapperDescriptorType,\n                    types.BuiltinFunctionType,\n                ),\n            )\n            and name not in disallowed_tensor_methods\n        ):\n            s.add(method)\n\n    # mlazos: these are functions which we handle specially in TensorVariable\n    s.add(torch.Tensor.__contains__)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n    s.add(torch.Tensor.register_hook)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n    return frozenset(s)\n\n\n\"\"\"\nReturn if a torch object is ATen op or torch.Tensor method.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef is_aten_op_or_tensor_method(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    return obj in get_tensor_method() or isinstance(\n        obj,\n        (torch._ops.OpOverloadPacket, torch._ops.OpOverload),\n    )\n\n\nclass FunctionIdSet:\n    \"\"\"\n    Track a set of `id()`s of objects which are either allowed or not\n    allowed to go into the generated FX graph.  Use to test for torch.*,\n    numpy.*, builtins.*, etc.\n\n    Support user modification to permit customization of what can be\n    added to the graph and what will cause a graph break.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    function_ids: Optional[set[int]] = None\n    function_names: Optional[dict[int, str]] = None\n\n    def __init__(\n        self, lazy_initializer: Callable[[], Union[dict[int, str], set[int]]]\n    ) -> None:\n        self.lazy_initializer = lazy_initializer\n\n    def __call__(self) -> set[int]:\n        if self.function_ids is None:\n            value = self.lazy_initializer()\n            if isinstance(value, dict):\n                self.function_ids = set(value.keys())\n                self.function_names = value\n            else:\n                assert isinstance(value, set)\n                self.function_ids = value\n        return self.function_ids\n\n    def get_name(self, idx: int, default: str) -> str:\n        self()  # lazy init\n        assert self.function_names is not None\n        return self.function_names.get(idx, default)\n\n    def add(self, idx: int) -> None:\n        function_ids = self()  # lazy init\n        function_ids.add(idx)\n\n    def remove(self, idx: int) -> None:\n        function_ids = self()\n        if idx in function_ids:\n            function_ids.remove(idx)\n\n    def __contains__(self, idx: int) -> bool:\n        return idx in self()\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _allowed_callable_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    rv: dict[int, str] = {}\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _disallowed_callable_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    rv: dict[int, str] = {}\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _nonstrict_trace_callable_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    rv: dict[int, str] = {}\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _builtin_function_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    # See also torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py, which removes items in _builtin_function_ids\n    rv = {\n        id(v): f\"builtins.{k}\"\n        for k, v in builtins.__dict__.items()\n        if not k.startswith(\"_\") and callable(v)\n    }\n    rv.update(\n        {\n            id(v): f\"operator.{k}\"\n            for k, v in operator.__dict__.items()\n            if not k.startswith(\"_\") and callable(v)\n        }\n    )\n    rv.update(\n        {\n            id(cast): \"typing.cast\",\n            id(copy.deepcopy): \"copy.deepcopy\",\n        }\n    )\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _polyfilled_function_ids() -> set[int]:\n    # See also @torch._dynamo.decorators.substitute_in_graph(...), which adds items in _polyfilled_function_ids\n    return set()\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _numpy_function_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    unsupported_funcs = {\n        \"seed\",\n        \"ranf\",\n        \"get_bit_generator\",\n        \"RandomState\",\n        \"set_bit_generator\",\n        \"sample\",\n    }\n\n    def is_supported(k: str, v: Any, mod: Any) -> bool:\n        if not callable(v):\n            return False\n        if not getattr(v, \"__module__\", None):\n            return True\n        if v.__module__ == mod.__name__:\n            return True\n        if (\n            v.__module__ == \"numpy.random.mtrand\"\n            and mod.__name__ == \"numpy.random\"\n            and k not in unsupported_funcs\n        ):\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    rv = {}\n    for mod in NP_SUPPORTED_MODULES:\n        for k, v in mod.__dict__.items():\n            if is_supported(k, v, mod):\n                rv[id(v)] = f\"{mod.__name__}.{k}\"\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _builtin_constant_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Collects constant builtins by eliminating callable items.\n    \"\"\"\n    rv = {\n        id(v): f\"builtins.{k}\"\n        for k, v in builtins.__dict__.items()\n        if not k.startswith(\"_\") and not callable(v)\n    }\n    return rv\n\n\n_lazy_module_init: dict[str, list[Callable[[], None]]] = defaultdict(list)\n\n\ndef add_module_init_func(name: str, init_func: Callable[[], None]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Register a module without eagerly importing it\"\"\"\n    # If the module is already imported, eagerly run init\n    assert \".\" not in name, f\"Expected a root module name, but got {name}\"\n    assert name not in _lazy_module_init\n    _lazy_module_init[name].append(init_func)\n\n\ndef _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj: object) -> None:\n    module = getattr(obj, \"__module__\", None)\n    if module is None:\n        return\n\n    base_module = module.split(\".\")[0]\n    init_funcs = _lazy_module_init.pop(base_module, None)\n    if init_funcs is not None:\n        for fn in init_funcs:\n            fn()\n\n\ndef is_callable_allowed(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return id(obj) in _allowed_callable_ids\n\n\ndef is_nonstrict_trace_callable(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return id(obj) in _nonstrict_trace_callable_ids\n\n\ndef is_callable_disallowed(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return id(obj) in _disallowed_callable_ids\n\n\ndef is_forbidden(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return inspect.getattr_static(obj, \"_dynamo_forbidden\", False)\n\n\ndef is_builtin_callable(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    # See also torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py, which removes items in _builtin_function_ids\n    return id(obj) in _builtin_function_ids\n\n\ndef is_builtin_constant(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    return id(obj) in _builtin_constant_ids\n\n\ndef is_polyfilled_callable(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    # See also @torch._dynamo.decorators.substitute_in_graph(...), which adds items in _polyfilled_function_ids\n    return id(obj) in _polyfilled_function_ids\n\n\ndef is_numpy(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    if np is None:\n        return False\n    return isinstance(obj, (np.ndarray, np.generic)) or id(obj) in _numpy_function_ids\n\n\ndef is_numpy_dtype(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    if np is None:\n        return False\n    return isinstance(obj, np.dtype)\n\n\ndef is_numpy_type_info(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    if np is None:\n        return False\n    return isinstance(obj, (np.finfo, np.iinfo))\n\n\nBUILTIN_SKIPLIST = (\n    abc,\n    copy,\n    random,\n    traceback,\n    linecache,\n)\n\n# third party libraries skiplist is defined by str, because users may not use these libraries.\n# we should use lazy import & skip in the future.\nTHIRDPARTY_SKIPLIST = (\n    \"fx2trt_oss\",\n    \"hypothesis\",\n    \"networkx\",\n    \"numpy\",\n    \"onnx\",\n    \"onnxruntime\",\n    \"onnx_tf\",\n    \"pandas\",\n    \"sklearn\",\n    \"tabulate\",\n    \"tensorflow\",\n    \"tensorrt\",\n    \"torch2trt\",\n    \"tqdm\",\n    \"tree\",\n    \"tvm\",\n    \"xarray\",\n)\n\n\ndef _as_posix_path(path: str) -> str:\n    posix_path = Path(os.path.normpath(path)).as_posix()\n    # os.path.normpath and pathlib.Path remove trailing slash, so we need to add it back\n    if path.endswith((os.path.sep, \"/\")):\n        posix_path += \"/\"\n    return posix_path\n\n\ndef _strip_init_py(s: str) -> str:\n    suffix = \"__init__.py\"\n    s = s.removesuffix(suffix)\n    return _as_posix_path(s)\n\n\ndef _module_dir(m: types.ModuleType) -> Optional[str]:\n    # Protect against a module not exporting __file__ - this can happen for\n    # frozen modules, for example.\n    file = getattr(m, \"__file__\", None)\n    return file and _strip_init_py(file)\n\n\n# These are legacy workarounds, don't add new modules to this list.\n# Please use the MOD_INLINELIST instead to force inline functions under particular modules.\n#\n# NB: The only thing that is different about MOD_INLINELIST and LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST\n# is the behavior of a function f2 in the module when called by a function f1\n# in a module in MOD_SKIPLIST (see MOD_SKIPLIST for more details)\n#\n# LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST is the same thing as Dynamo's behavior on a module that\n# is not in any *_INLINELIST or *_SKIPLIST.\n# That being said, we prefer people to add things to MOD_INLINELIST over\n# LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST because it is less likely to break existing tests.\nLEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST = {\n    \"torch._dynamo.external_utils\",\n    \"torch._export.db.examples\",\n    \"torch._export.wrappers\",\n    \"torch._functorch.apis\",\n    \"torch._functorch.deprecated\",\n    \"torch.nn.attention.flex_attention\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.stubs\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.export_utils\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.qat_utils\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.representation.rewrite\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.utils\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.quantizer.xnnpack_quantizer\",\n    \"torch.export.unflatten\",\n}\n\nif torch.distributed.is_available():\n    LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST |= {\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor._api\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.device_mesh\",\n        \"torch.distributed.device_mesh\",\n        \"torch.distributed.algorithms._checkpoint.checkpoint_wrapper\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.parallel._data_parallel_utils\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.parallel._utils\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.parallel.style\",\n        # we have to add replicate to LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST to ensure\n        # the forward_hook won't be ignored.\n        \"torch.distributed._composable.replicate\",\n    }\n    if not config.skip_fsdp_hooks:\n        LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST.add(\"torch.distributed.fsdp._fully_shard\")\n\n# Force inline functions under these modules, even they are in *_SKIPLIST.\n# We are using python module name instead of file or directory object to avoid circular dependency.\n# Please keep this sorted alphabetically.\n#\n# Btw, it is not \"ideal\" for something to be in MOD_INLINELIST. If Dynamo\n# fully supports a module, then the ideal case is that it is not in\n# any *_INLINELIST or *_SKIPLIST: then, the behavior of Dynamo is that\n# it will always inline into functions in the module.\nMOD_INLINELIST = [\n    \"torch._decomp\",\n    \"torch._dynamo._trace_wrapped_higher_order_op\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.comptime\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.polyfills\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.test_case\",\n    \"torch._export.non_strict_utils\",\n    \"torch._functorch._aot_autograd.subclass_parametrization\",\n    \"torch._functorch.autograd_function\",\n    \"torch._functorch.eager_transforms\",\n    \"torch._functorch.functional_call\",\n    \"torch._functorch.pyfunctorch\",\n    \"torch._functorch.vmap\",\n    \"torch._inductor.test_operators\",\n    \"torch._library.autograd\",\n    \"torch._library.custom_ops\",\n    \"torch._ops\",\n    \"torch._prims\",\n    \"torch._refs\",\n    \"torch._tensor\",\n    \"torch.amp.autocast_mode\",\n    \"torch.ao.nn\",\n    \"torch.autograd.function\",\n    \"torch.backends.cuda\",\n    \"torch.cuda.amp.autocast_mode\",\n    \"torch.distributions\",\n    \"torch.export._tree_utils\",\n    \"torch.export._unlift\",\n    \"torch.export._wrapper_utils\",\n    \"torch.fx._pytree\",\n    \"torch.fx._symbolic_trace\",\n    \"torch.fx.experimental.proxy_tensor\",\n    \"torch.fx.passes.shape_prop\",\n    \"torch.fx.traceback\",\n    \"torch.nn\",\n    \"torch.overrides\",\n    \"torch.random\",\n    \"torch.return_types\",\n    \"torch.sparse\",\n    \"torch.testing\",\n    \"torch.utils._content_store\",\n    \"torch.utils._contextlib\",\n    \"torch.utils._cxx_pytree\",\n    \"torch.utils._device\",\n    \"torch.utils._foreach_utils\",\n    \"torch.utils._python_dispatch\",\n    \"torch.utils._pytree\",\n    \"torch.utils.hooks\",\n]\nassert sorted(set(MOD_INLINELIST)) == MOD_INLINELIST\nMOD_INLINELIST = set(MOD_INLINELIST)\n\n\nif torch.distributed.is_available():\n    MOD_INLINELIST.add(\"torch.distributed\")\n    if not config.skip_fsdp_hooks:\n        MOD_INLINELIST.add(\"torch.distributed.fsdp._fully_shard\")\n\n\n# By default, all functions under these modules are skipped.\n# All the other knobs\n# (torch_name_rule_map, MOD_INLINELIST, LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST)\n# take precedence over this list; e.g. if a function is in\n# MOD_INLINELIST and MOD_SKIPLIST, then it will be inlined.\n# See \"A note on skip/inline rules\" for more details.\n#\n# The skip is NOT recursive. If a function f1 in a module in MOD_SKIPLIST\n# calls out to another function f2 in some other module, then Dynamo's\n# behavior (skip/inline) depends on what we've marked f2 as:\n# - if f2 is a function in a module in MOD_SKIPLIST, then we skip f2\n# - if f2 is a function in a module in MOD_INLINELIST, then we skip f2\n# - if f2 is a function in a module in LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST, then we inline f2\n# - if f2 is a function in a module not in any *_LIST, then we inline f2\nMOD_SKIPLIST = [\n    \"torch._VF\",\n    \"torch.__future__\",\n    \"torch.__init__\",\n    \"torch._awaits\",\n    \"torch._classes\",\n    \"torch._compile\",\n    \"torch._custom_op\",\n    \"torch._custom_ops\",\n    \"torch._decomp\",\n    \"torch._dispatch\",\n    \"torch._dynamo\",\n    \"torch._export\",\n    \"torch._functorch\",\n    \"torch._guards\",\n    \"torch._higher_order_ops.effects\",\n    \"torch._higher_order_ops.torchbind\",\n    \"torch._higher_order_ops.wrap\",\n    \"torch._inductor\",\n    \"torch._jit_internal\",\n    \"torch._lazy\",\n    \"torch._library\",\n    \"torch._linalg_utils\",\n    \"torch._lobpcg\",\n    \"torch._logging\",\n    \"torch._lowrank\",\n    \"torch._meta_registrations\",\n    \"torch._namedtensor_internals\",\n    \"torch._numpy\",\n    \"torch._ops\",\n    \"torch._prims\",\n    \"torch._prims_common\",\n    \"torch._python_dispatcher\",\n    \"torch._refs\",\n    \"torch._strobelight\",\n    \"torch._subclasses\",\n    \"torch._tensor\",\n    \"torch._tensor_str\",\n    \"torch._thread_safe_fork\",\n    \"torch._utils\",\n    \"torch._utils_internal\",\n    \"torch._vmap_internals\",\n    \"torch._weights_only_unpickler\",\n    \"torch.accelerator\",\n    \"torch.amp\",\n    \"torch.ao\",\n    \"torch.autograd\",\n    \"torch.backends\",\n    \"torch.compiler\",\n    \"torch.contrib\",\n    \"torch.cpu\",\n    \"torch.cuda\",\n    \"torch.distributed\",\n    \"torch.distributions\",\n    \"torch.export\",\n    \"torch.fb\",\n    \"torch.fft\",\n    \"torch.functional\",\n    \"torch.futures\",\n    \"torch.fx\",\n    \"torch.hub\",\n    \"torch.jit\",\n    \"torch.library\",\n    \"torch.linalg\",\n    \"torch.masked\",\n    \"torch.monitor\",\n    \"torch.mps\",\n    \"torch.mtia\",\n    \"torch.multiprocessing\",\n    \"torch.nested\",\n    \"torch.nn\",\n    \"torch.onnx\",\n    \"torch.overrides\",\n    \"torch.package\",\n    \"torch.profiler\",\n    \"torch.quantization\",\n    \"torch.quasirandom\",\n    \"torch.random\",\n    \"torch.serialization\",\n    \"torch.signal\",\n    \"torch.sparse\",\n    \"torch.special\",\n    \"torch.storage\",\n    \"torch.testing\",\n    \"torch.types\",\n    \"torch.utils\",\n    \"torch.xpu\",\n]\n\nassert sorted(set(MOD_SKIPLIST)) == MOD_SKIPLIST\nMOD_SKIPLIST = set(MOD_SKIPLIST)\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_legacy_mod_inlinelist() -> set[str]:\n    torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n    if torch_dir is None:\n        return set()\n    inlinelist = {\n        _as_posix_path(torch_dir + m[len(\"torch.\") :].replace(\".\", \"/\"))\n        for m in LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST\n    }\n    return inlinelist\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_mod_inlinelist() -> set[str]:\n    torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n    if torch_dir is None:\n        return set()\n    inlinelist = {\n        _as_posix_path(torch_dir + m[len(\"torch.\") :].replace(\".\", \"/\"))\n        for m in MOD_INLINELIST\n    }\n    return inlinelist\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_mod_skiplist() -> set[str]:\n    torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n    if torch_dir is None:\n        return set()\n    skiplist = {\n        _as_posix_path(torch_dir + m[len(\"torch.\") :].replace(\".\", \"/\"))\n        for m in MOD_SKIPLIST\n    }\n    return skiplist\n\n\n# skip some standard python builtin libs\nSKIP_DIRS = [\n    \"<frozen importlib\",\n    \"<frozen abc\",\n    \"<__array_function__ internals>\",\n    _as_posix_path(_config_module.__file__),\n    \"triton/backends\",\n]\nSKIP_DIRS.extend(map(_as_posix_path, filter(None, map(_module_dir, BUILTIN_SKIPLIST))))\n\nSKIP_DIRS_RE = re.compile(r\"match nothing^\")\n\n# Skip fbcode paths(including torch.package paths) containing\n# one of the following strings.\nFBCODE_SKIP_DIRS: set[str] = set()\n\nFBCODE_SKIP_DIRS_RE = re.compile(f\".*({'|'.join(map(re.escape, FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS))})\")\n\n# Remove this after fbcode is fully migrated to tracing through torchrec.\nFBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS = {\n    \"torchrec/distributed\",\n    \"torchrec/fb/distributed\",\n    \"caffe2/torch/fb/sparsenn/pooled_embeddings_modules.py\",\n}\n\nFBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS_RE = re.compile(\n    f\".*({'|'.join(re.escape(_as_posix_path(d)) for d in FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS)})\"\n)\n\n# TODO(yanboliang, anijain2305) - There are a few concerns that we should\n# resolve\n# 1) Audit if torchrec/distributed is even required in FBCODE_SKIPS_DIR\n# 2) To inline just one file but skip others in a directory, we could use\n# manual_torch_name_rule_map but this one is hard because FBCODE can add unusual\n# names like torch_package.\n# So, this is a stop gap solution till then.\nFBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS = {\n    \"torchrec/distributed/types.py\",\n}\nFBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS_RE = re.compile(\n    f\".*({'|'.join(re.escape(_as_posix_path(d)) for d in FBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS)})\"\n)\n\n# torch.optim is a special case,\n# we usually want to inline it, but the directory\n# structure does not match the module structure\n# and we want to skip the functions in optim/lr_scheduler.py\n# this has precedence over all other rules in check_file\nFORCE_SKIP_FILES = {f\"{_module_dir(torch)}optim/lr_scheduler.py\"}\n\n\ndef _recompile_re() -> None:\n    global SKIP_DIRS_RE\n    SKIP_DIRS_RE = re.compile(\n        rf\"^[^\\s<]*({'|'.join(re.escape(_as_posix_path(d)) for d in SKIP_DIRS)})\"\n    )\n\n\ndef add(import_name: str) -> None:\n    if isinstance(import_name, types.ModuleType):\n        return add(import_name.__name__)\n    assert isinstance(import_name, str)\n    from importlib.util import find_spec\n\n    module_spec = find_spec(import_name)\n    if not module_spec:\n        return\n    origin = module_spec.origin\n    if origin is None:\n        return\n    SKIP_DIRS.append(_strip_init_py(origin))\n    _recompile_re()\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass SkipResult:\n    skipped: bool\n    reason: Optional[str]\n\n\ndef check_file(filename: Optional[str], is_inlined_call: bool = False) -> SkipResult:\n    \"\"\"Should skip this file?\"\"\"\n    if filename is None:\n        return SkipResult(True, \"filename is None\")\n    filename = _as_posix_path(filename)\n    if filename in FORCE_SKIP_FILES:\n        return SkipResult(True, \"FORCE_SKIP_FILES\")\n\n    if any(filename.startswith(d) for d in get_legacy_mod_inlinelist()):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            \"LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST\",\n        )\n    if is_inlined_call and is_torch_inline_allowed(filename):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            \"MOD_INLINELIST\",\n        )\n    if (\n        is_fbcode()\n        and FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS\n        and bool(FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n        and not bool(FBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(\n            True,\n            \"FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS\",\n        )\n\n    if (\n        is_fbcode()\n        and config.skip_torchrec\n        and FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS\n        and bool(FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n        and not bool(FBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS\")\n\n    unittest_dir = _module_dir(unittest)\n    if (\n        unittest_dir is not None\n        and filename.startswith(unittest_dir)\n        and not torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"unittest\")\n\n    if bool(SKIP_DIRS_RE.match(filename)):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"SKIP_DIRS\")\n\n    if any(filename.startswith(d) for d in get_mod_skiplist()):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"MOD_SKIPLIST\")\n    return SkipResult(False, \"inlined by default\")\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass FunctionInfo:\n    py_obj: Optional[object]\n    name: Optional[str]\n    filename: str\n    code: Optional[types.CodeType]\n\n\n\"\"\"\nThis is the main entry point to determine whether an object (function) should be inlined or skipped.\nLet's illustrate the logic with an example:\n    @torch.compile\n    def f1(x, y):\n        ......\n        f2(x, y)\n        ......\n\n    def f2(x, y):\n        ......\n        f3(x, y)\n        ......\n\n    def f3(x, y):\n        ......\n\nThere are mainly three call sites of check/check_verbose:\n* The compile region entrance (like function f1), the corresponding code is located at eval_frame.py.\n* When tracing the recursively called functions (like function f2 and f3).\n    * Dynamo decides inline/skip every time it encounters a new recursively function call, and the call site\n      is in InliningInstructionTranslator.check_inlineable of symbolic_convert.py.\n    * If f2 is skipped by Dynamo, when evaluating the frame of f3, Dynamo need the inline/skip check again\n      and the call site is in catch_errors_wrapper.catch_errors of convert_frame.py.\n* For global variables and function arguments, Dynamo needs to decide if they are wrapped as SkipFunctionVariable in builder.py.\n\n`is_inlined_call` is used to indicate if the current function call is inlined (f2 is inlined call if it passes check)\nor not (f3 is not inlined call if f2 is skipped). Inside of the `check_verbose` function, there are more rules\nto be checked if this `is_inlined_call`.\nThe reason to have this flag is that if the upper level function call (e.g, f2) is skipped,\nwe don't want to inline the lower level function call (e.g, f3) by default.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef check_verbose(obj: Any, is_inlined_call: bool = False) -> SkipResult:\n    if isinstance(\n        obj,\n        (\n            UserFunctionVariable,\n            UserMethodVariable,\n            NestedUserFunctionVariable,\n            LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n            LocalGeneratorObjectVariable,\n        ),\n    ):\n        try:\n            py_obj = obj.get_function()\n        except NotImplementedError:\n            py_obj = None\n        fi = FunctionInfo(py_obj, obj.get_name(), obj.get_filename(), obj.get_code())\n    elif isinstance(obj, types.CodeType):\n        fi = FunctionInfo(None, obj.co_name, obj.co_filename, obj)\n    elif isinstance(obj, (types.FunctionType, types.MethodType)):\n        filename = getfile(obj)\n        assert filename is not None\n        fi = FunctionInfo(\n            obj,\n            obj.__name__,\n            filename,\n            obj.__code__,  # type: ignore[union-attr] # FIXME Add MethodType.__code__ to typeshed\n        )\n    else:\n        filename = getfile(obj)\n        assert filename is not None\n        fi = FunctionInfo(obj, None, filename, None)\n\n    # Consulte the central trace rules defined in torch._dynamo.trace_rules.\n    reasons: set[str] = set()\n    rule = lookup_inner(fi.py_obj, fi.name, fi.filename, is_inlined_call, reasons)\n    assert rule is not None\n    if issubclass(\n        rule,\n        (\n            UserFunctionVariable,\n            LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n            PolyfilledFunctionVariable,\n        ),\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            f\"inlined according trace_rules.lookup {reasons.pop()}\",\n        )\n    elif issubclass(rule, TorchInGraphFunctionVariable):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            f\"registered in torch_obj_rule {reasons.pop()}\",\n        )\n    else:\n        assert rule == SkipFunctionVariable, rule\n        return SkipResult(\n            True,\n            f\"skipped according trace_rules.lookup {reasons.pop()}\",\n        )\n\n\ndef check(obj: Any, is_inlined_call: bool = False) -> bool:\n    return check_verbose(obj, is_inlined_call).skipped\n\n\n# skip common third party libs\nfor _name in THIRDPARTY_SKIPLIST:\n    add(_name)\n\n_recompile_re()\n\n\ndef is_torch_inline_allowed(filename: str) -> bool:\n    return any(filename.startswith(d) for d in get_mod_inlinelist())\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef dynamo_dir() -> Optional[str]:\n    import torch._dynamo\n\n    return _module_dir(torch._dynamo)\n\n\ndef is_torch(filename: str) -> bool:\n    dynamo_path = dynamo_dir()\n    if dynamo_path is not None and filename.startswith(dynamo_path):\n        return False\n    torch_path = _module_dir(torch)\n    return torch_path is not None and filename.startswith(torch_path)\n\n\n\"\"\"\nMain entry point for looking up the trace rule (the Dynamo variable) for a given callable object.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef lookup_callable(obj: Callable[..., Any]) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    if not hashable(obj):\n        return None\n    # Custom allow/disallow in graph takes precedence over the general lookup.\n    if is_callable_disallowed(obj):\n        return SkipFunctionVariable\n    if is_callable_allowed(obj):\n        return TorchInGraphFunctionVariable\n    if is_polyfilled_callable(obj):\n        return PolyfilledFunctionVariable\n    if is_builtin_callable(obj):\n        return BuiltinVariable\n    return None\n\n\n\"\"\"\nMain entry point for looking up the trace rule (the Dynamo variable) for a given function object.\nE.g, the lookup result of `torch.sin` is `TorchInGraphFunctionVariable`.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef lookup(obj: Any) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    return lookup_inner(obj)\n\n\n# also takes config.dont_skip_tracing into account\ndef lookup_inner(\n    obj: Any,\n    name: Optional[str] = None,\n    filename: Optional[str] = None,\n    is_direct_call: bool = True,\n    reasons: Union[None, set[str]] = None,\n) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    result = _lookup_inner(\n        obj,\n        name=name,\n        filename=filename,\n        is_direct_call=is_direct_call,\n        reasons=reasons,\n    )\n    # There are still some modules we should absolutely NOT trace into - e.g. most of torch._dynamo,\n    # as this can result in really weird tracing behaviors.\n    # Note that if a torch._dynamo function is already not skipped (e.g. functions in external_utils.py),\n    # then this branch does not apply.\n    if config.dont_skip_tracing and result is SkipFunctionVariable:\n        if filename is None:\n            filename = getfile(obj)\n        assert filename is not None\n        filename = _as_posix_path(filename)\n        torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n        if torch_dir is not None:\n            dynamo_path = _as_posix_path(torch_dir) + \"_dynamo\"\n            if filename.startswith(dynamo_path) and not filename.endswith(\n                \"test_dont_skip_tracing_functions.py\"\n            ):\n                return SkipFunctionVariable\n        if reasons is not None:\n            reasons.add(\n                \"Attempted skip but we are ignoring skips due to torch._dynamo.config.dont_skip_tracing\"\n            )\n        return UserFunctionVariable\n    return result\n\n\ndef _lookup_inner(\n    obj: Any,\n    name: Optional[str] = None,\n    filename: Optional[str] = None,\n    is_direct_call: bool = True,\n    reasons: Optional[set[str]] = None,\n) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    # Step 1: lookup obj's tracing rule in `torch_name_rule_map`.\n    # The rules defined in `torch_name_rule_map` mainly includes two parts:\n    # - Manually defined rules for any functions.\n    # - The list of torch in graph functions.\n    try:\n        can_hash = hashable(obj)\n    except Exception:\n        can_hash = False\n    if not can_hash:\n        if reasons is not None:\n            reasons.add(\"obj is not hashable\")\n        return None\n    if obj is not None:\n        if is_aten_op_or_tensor_method(obj):\n            return TorchInGraphFunctionVariable\n        rule = get_torch_obj_rule_map().get(obj, None)\n        if rule is not None:\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"get_torch_obj_rule_map\")\n            return rule\n    elif name is not None and filename is not None and not is_direct_call:\n        if name.startswith(TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX):\n            rule = get_torch_obj_rule_map().get(\n                filename + \"#\" + TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX, None\n            )\n        else:\n            rule = get_torch_obj_rule_map().get(filename + \"#\" + name, None)\n        if rule is not None:\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"get_torch_obj_rule_map\")\n            return rule\n    elif name == \"<listcomp>\":\n        if reasons is not None:\n            reasons.add(\"inlining frame from list comprehension\")\n        return UserFunctionVariable\n\n    # Step 2: lookup obj's tracing rule by function name.\n    if is_direct_call:\n        if name == \"patched_init\":\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"func name is patched_init\")\n            return SkipFunctionVariable\n        elif name == \"__torch_function__\" or (\n            obj and getattr(obj, \"__name__\", None) == \"__torch_function__\"\n        ):\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"func name is __torch_function__\")\n            return UserFunctionVariable\n\n    if not is_direct_call:\n        if name == \"__getattr__\":\n            # is_direct_call = False indicates that this is the top-level frame\n            # being traced (i.e., it is not inlined and not called from\n            # InliningInstructionTranslator).  Tracing __getattr__ at the top\n            # level is unlikely because we inline it for\n            # UserDefinedObjectVariable. This scenario occurs only for\n            # UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, where Dynamo directly calls\n            # __getattr__ during trace time, generating LOAD_ATTR bytecode\n            # without going through the underlying __getattr__ data structures.\n            # When this optimized bytecode is executed, Dynamo is triggered\n            # again on the __getattr__ call. Therefore, we skip Dynamo tracing\n            # in this case.\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\n                    \"Tracing __getattr__ as the top level frame, unsuitable for tracing.\"\n                )\n            return SkipFunctionVariable\n\n    # Step 3: lookup obj's tracing rule by filename.\n    if filename is None:\n        filename = getfile(obj)\n\n    skip_result = check_file(filename, is_direct_call)\n    if reasons is not None and skip_result.reason is not None:\n        reasons.add(skip_result.reason)\n    if skip_result.skipped:\n        return SkipFunctionVariable\n    else:\n        return UserFunctionVariable\n\n\ndef clear_lru_cache() -> None:\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_torch_obj_rule_map.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_tensor_method.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_legacy_mod_inlinelist.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_mod_inlinelist.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.dynamo_dir.cache_clear()\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "         \"torch._add_batch_dim\",",
        "         \"torch._add_relu_\",",
        "         \"torch._add_relu\",",

        "         \"torch._aminmax\",",
        "         \"torch._amp_foreach_non_finite_check_and_unscale_\",",
        "         \"torch._amp_update_scale_\","

  "newString": "        Tensor._is_zerotensor,\n        Tensor._is_all_true,\n        Tensor._is_any_true,\n        Tensor.to_padded_tensor,",
  "oldString": "        Tensor._is_zerotensor,\n        Tensor._is_all_true,\n        Tensor._is_any_true,\n        Tensor._addmm_activation,\n        Tensor.to_padded_tensor,",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nPython implementation of ``__torch_function__``\n\nWhile most of the torch API and handling for ``__torch_function__`` happens\nat the C++ level, some of the torch API is written in Python so we need\npython-level handling for ``__torch_function__`` overrides as well. The main\ndeveloper-facing functionality in this file are handle_torch_function and\nhas_torch_function. See torch/functional.py and test/test_overrides.py\nfor usage examples.\n\nNote\n----\nheavily inspired by NumPy's ``__array_function__`` (see:\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/24015 and\nhttps://www.numpy.org/neps/nep-0018-array-function-protocol.html\n)\n\nIf changing this file in a way that can affect ``__torch_function__`` overhead,\nplease report the benchmarks in ``benchmarks/overrides_benchmark``. See the\ninstructions in the ``README.md`` in that directory.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport __future__  # noqa: F404\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport functools\nimport sys\nimport types\nimport warnings\nfrom collections.abc import Callable, Iterable\nfrom functools import wraps\nfrom typing import Any, TypeVar\nfrom typing_extensions import ParamSpec\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch._C import (\n    _add_docstr,\n    _get_function_stack_at,\n    _has_torch_function,\n    _has_torch_function_unary,\n    _has_torch_function_variadic,\n    _is_torch_function_mode_enabled,\n    _len_torch_function_stack,\n    _pop_torch_function_stack,\n    _push_on_torch_function_stack,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"get_ignored_functions\",\n    \"get_overridable_functions\",\n    \"get_testing_overrides\",\n    \"handle_torch_function\",\n    \"has_torch_function\",\n    \"resolve_name\",\n    \"is_tensor_like\",\n    \"is_tensor_method_or_property\",\n    \"wrap_torch_function\",\n    \"enable_reentrant_dispatch\",\n]\n\n_P = ParamSpec(\"_P\")\n_R = TypeVar(\"_R\")\n\n\ndef _disable_user_warnings(\n    func: Callable[_P, _R],\n    regex: str = \".*is deprecated, please use.*\",\n    module: str = \"torch\",\n) -> Callable[_P, _R]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Decorator that temporarily disables ``UserWarning``s for the given ``module`` if the warning message matches the\n    given ``regex`` pattern.\n\n    Arguments\n    ---------\n    func : function\n        Function to disable the warnings for.\n    regex : str\n        A regex pattern compilable by ``re.compile``. This is used to match the ``UserWarning`` message.\n    module : str\n        The python module to which the filtering should be restricted.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    function\n        The wrapped function.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @wraps(func)\n    def wrapper(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _R:\n        with warnings.catch_warnings():\n            warnings.filterwarnings(\n                \"ignore\", category=UserWarning, message=regex, module=module\n            )\n            return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    return wrapper\n\n\n@functools.cache\n@_disable_user_warnings\ndef get_ignored_functions() -> set[Callable]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return public functions that cannot be overridden by ``__torch_function__``.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    set[Callable]\n        A tuple of functions that are publicly available in the torch API but cannot\n        be overridden with ``__torch_function__``. Mostly this is because none of the\n        arguments of these functions are tensors or tensor-likes.\n\n    Examples\n    --------\n    >>> torch.Tensor.as_subclass in torch.overrides.get_ignored_functions()\n    True\n    >>> torch.add in torch.overrides.get_ignored_functions()\n    False\n    \"\"\"\n    Tensor = torch.Tensor\n    functions = {\n        torch.typename,\n        torch.is_tensor,\n        torch.is_storage,\n        torch.set_default_tensor_type,\n        torch.set_default_device,\n        torch.get_default_device,\n        torch.set_rng_state,\n        torch.get_rng_state,\n        torch.manual_seed,\n        torch.initial_seed,\n        torch.seed,\n        torch.save,\n        torch.load,\n        torch.set_printoptions,\n        torch.fork,\n        torch.get_default_dtype,\n        torch.get_num_interop_threads,\n        torch.get_num_threads,\n        torch.init_num_threads,\n        torch.import_ir_module,\n        torch.import_ir_module_from_buffer,\n        torch.is_anomaly_enabled,\n        torch.is_anomaly_check_nan_enabled,\n        torch.is_grad_enabled,\n        torch.merge_type_from_type_comment,\n        torch.parse_ir,\n        torch.parse_schema,\n        torch.parse_type_comment,\n        torch.set_anomaly_enabled,\n        torch.set_flush_denormal,\n        torch.set_num_interop_threads,\n        torch.set_num_threads,\n        torch.wait,\n        torch.as_tensor,\n        torch.from_numpy,\n        torch.tensor,\n        torch.default_generator,\n        torch.has_cuda,\n        torch.has_cudnn,\n        torch.has_lapack,\n        torch.device,\n        torch.dtype,\n        torch.finfo,\n        torch.has_mkl,\n        torch.has_mps,\n        torch.has_mkldnn,\n        torch.has_openmp,\n        torch.iinfo,\n        torch.memory_format,\n        torch.qscheme,\n        torch.set_grad_enabled,\n        torch.no_grad,\n        torch.enable_grad,\n        torch.inference_mode,\n        torch.is_inference_mode_enabled,\n        torch.layout,\n        torch.align_tensors,\n        torch.arange,\n        torch.as_strided,\n        torch.bartlett_window,\n        torch.blackman_window,\n        torch.broadcast_shapes,\n        torch.can_cast,\n        torch.compile,\n        torch.cudnn_affine_grid_generator,\n        torch.cudnn_batch_norm,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution_transpose,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution_relu,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution_add_relu,\n        torch.cudnn_grid_sampler,\n        torch.cudnn_is_acceptable,\n        torch.empty,\n        torch.empty_permuted,\n        torch.empty_strided,\n        torch.empty_quantized,\n        torch.export.export,\n        torch.export.load,\n        torch.export.register_dataclass,\n        torch.export.save,\n        torch.eye,\n        torch.fft.fftfreq,\n        torch.fft.rfftfreq,\n        torch.from_file,\n        torch.full,\n        torch.fill,\n        torch.hamming_window,\n        torch.hann_window,\n        torch.kaiser_window,\n        torch.linspace,\n        torch.logspace,\n        torch.mkldnn_adaptive_avg_pool2d,\n        torch.mkldnn_convolution,\n        torch.mkldnn_max_pool2d,\n        torch.mkldnn_max_pool3d,\n        torch.mkldnn_linear_backward_weights,\n        torch.mkldnn_rnn_layer,\n        torch.normal,\n        torch.ones,\n        torch.promote_types,\n        torch.rand,\n        torch.rand_like,\n        torch.randn,\n        torch.randn_like,\n        torch.randint,\n        torch.randint_like,\n        torch.randperm,\n        torch.range,\n        torch.result_type,\n        torch.scalar_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_coo_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_compressed_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_csr_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_csc_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_bsr_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_bsc_tensor,\n        torch.sym_constrain_range,\n        torch.sym_constrain_range_for_size,\n        torch.sym_fresh_size,\n        torch.tril_indices,\n        torch.triu_indices,\n        torch.vander,\n        torch.zeros,\n        torch._jit_internal.boolean_dispatch,\n        torch.nn.functional.assert_int_or_pair,\n        torch.nn.functional.upsample,\n        torch.nn.functional.upsample_bilinear,\n        torch.nn.functional.upsample_nearest,\n        torch.nn.functional.has_torch_function,\n        torch.nn.functional.has_torch_function_unary,\n        torch.nn.functional.has_torch_function_variadic,\n        torch.nn.functional.handle_torch_function,\n        torch.nn.functional.grouped_mm,\n        torch.nn.functional.scaled_grouped_mm,\n        torch.nn.functional.scaled_mm,\n        torch.nn.functional.sigmoid,\n        torch.nn.functional.hardsigmoid,\n        torch.nn.functional.tanh,\n        torch.nn.functional._canonical_mask,\n        torch.nn.functional._none_or_dtype,\n        # Doesn't actually take or return tensor arguments\n        torch.nn.init.calculate_gain,\n        # These are deprecated; don't test them\n        torch.nn.init.uniform,\n        torch.nn.init.normal,\n        torch.nn.init.constant,\n        torch.nn.init.eye,\n        torch.nn.init.dirac,\n        torch.nn.init.xavier_uniform,\n        torch.nn.init.xavier_normal,\n        torch.nn.init.kaiming_uniform,\n        torch.nn.init.kaiming_normal,\n        torch.nn.init.orthogonal,\n        torch.nn.init.sparse,\n        torch.nested.to_padded_tensor,\n        has_torch_function,\n        handle_torch_function,\n        torch.set_autocast_enabled,\n        torch.is_autocast_enabled,\n        torch.set_autocast_dtype,\n        torch.get_autocast_dtype,\n        torch.clear_autocast_cache,\n        torch.set_autocast_cpu_enabled,\n        torch.is_autocast_cpu_enabled,\n        torch.set_autocast_xla_enabled,\n        torch.is_autocast_xla_enabled,\n        torch.set_autocast_ipu_enabled,\n        torch.is_autocast_ipu_enabled,\n        torch.set_autocast_cpu_dtype,\n        torch.get_autocast_cpu_dtype,\n        torch.set_autocast_ipu_dtype,\n        torch.get_autocast_ipu_dtype,\n        torch.get_autocast_gpu_dtype,\n        torch.set_autocast_gpu_dtype,\n        torch.get_autocast_xla_dtype,\n        torch.set_autocast_xla_dtype,\n        torch.autocast_increment_nesting,\n        torch.autocast_decrement_nesting,\n        torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled,\n        torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\n        torch.nn.functional.hardswish,\n        torch.is_vulkan_available,\n        torch.are_deterministic_algorithms_enabled,\n        torch.use_deterministic_algorithms,\n        torch.is_deterministic_algorithms_warn_only_enabled,\n        torch.set_deterministic_debug_mode,\n        torch.get_device_module,\n        torch.get_deterministic_debug_mode,\n        torch.set_float32_matmul_precision,\n        torch.get_float32_matmul_precision,\n        torch.unify_type_list,\n        torch.is_warn_always_enabled,\n        torch.set_warn_always,\n        torch.vitals_enabled,\n        torch.set_vital,\n        torch.read_vitals,\n        torch.vmap,\n        torch.cond,\n        torch.frombuffer,\n        torch.asarray,\n        torch._functional_sym_constrain_range,\n        torch._make_dep_token,\n        Tensor.__delitem__,\n        Tensor.__dir__,\n        Tensor.__getattribute__,\n        Tensor.__init__,\n        Tensor.__iter__,\n        Tensor.__init_subclass__,\n        Tensor.__delattr__,\n        Tensor.__setattr__,\n        Tensor.__torch_function__,\n        Tensor.__torch_dispatch__,\n        Tensor.__new__,\n        Tensor.__class__,\n        Tensor.__subclasshook__,\n        Tensor.__hash__,\n        Tensor.as_subclass,\n        Tensor.eig,\n        Tensor.lstsq,\n        Tensor.reinforce,\n        Tensor.new,\n        Tensor.new_tensor,\n        Tensor.new_empty,\n        Tensor.new_empty_strided,\n        Tensor.new_zeros,\n        Tensor.new_ones,\n        Tensor.new_full,\n        Tensor._make_subclass,\n        Tensor.solve,\n        Tensor.symeig,\n        Tensor.stride,\n        Tensor.unflatten,\n        Tensor.to_sparse_coo,\n        Tensor.to_sparse_csr,\n        Tensor.to_sparse_csc,\n        Tensor.to_sparse_bsr,\n        Tensor.to_sparse_bsc,\n        Tensor._to_sparse,\n        Tensor._to_sparse_csr,\n        Tensor._to_sparse_csc,\n        Tensor._to_sparse_bsr,\n        Tensor._to_sparse_bsc,\n        Tensor._typed_storage,\n        Tensor._reduce_ex_internal,\n        Tensor._fix_weakref,\n        Tensor._view_func,\n        Tensor._view_func_unsafe,\n        Tensor._rev_view_func_unsafe,\n        Tensor._dtensor__new__,\n        Tensor._make_wrapper_subclass,\n        Tensor._python_dispatch.__get__,\n        Tensor._has_symbolic_sizes_strides.__get__,\n        Tensor._conj,\n        Tensor._conj_physical,\n        Tensor._lazy_clone,\n        Tensor._neg_view,\n        Tensor._is_zerotensor,\n        Tensor._is_all_true,\n        Tensor._is_any_true,\n        Tensor._addmm_activation,\n        Tensor.to_padded_tensor,\n        Tensor._use_count,\n    }\n\n    if sys.version_info >= (3, 14):\n        functions.add(Tensor.__annotate__)\n\n    return functions\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_default_nowrap_functions() -> set[Callable]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return public functions that do not wrap in a subclass when invoked by\n    the default ``Tensor.__torch_function__`` that preserves subclasses.  Typically,\n    these functions represent field accesses (i.e., retrieving a Tensor that\n    is stored somewhere on the Tensor) as opposed to computation.  Users of\n    these functions expect object identity to be preserved over multiple accesses\n    (e.g., ``a.grad is a.grad``) which cannot be upheld if we're wrapping on\n    the fly every time (furthermore, the tensor stored here might already be\n    the subclass, in which case wrapping really ought not to happen).\n\n    Not ALL property accessors have this property; for example ``Tensor.T`` actually\n    just creates a new transposed tensor on the fly, and so we SHOULD interpose on\n    these calls (you need to check the implementation of the function to see if\n    this is the case or not).  Additionally, if a property accessor doesn't return a Tensor,\n    it doesn't have to be on this list (though it is harmless if it is).\n    \"\"\"\n    Tensor = torch.Tensor\n    return {\n        Tensor._base.__get__,\n        Tensor.grad.__get__,\n        Tensor._grad.__get__,\n    }\n\n\n@functools.cache\n@_disable_user_warnings\ndef get_testing_overrides() -> dict[Callable, Callable]:\n    \"\"\"Return a dict containing dummy overrides for all overridable functions\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Dict[Callable, Callable]\n        A dictionary that maps overridable functions in the PyTorch API to\n        lambda functions that have the same signature as the real function\n        and unconditionally return -1. These lambda functions are useful\n        for testing API coverage for a type that defines ``__torch_function__``.\n\n    Examples\n    --------\n    >>> import inspect\n    >>> my_add = torch.overrides.get_testing_overrides()[torch.add]\n    >>> inspect.signature(my_add)\n    <Signature (input, other, out=None)>\n    \"\"\"\n    # Every function in the PyTorchAPI that can be overridden needs an entry\n    # in this dict.\n    #\n    # Optimally we would use inspect to get the function signature and define\n    # the lambda function procedurally but that is blocked by generating\n    # function signatures for native kernels that can be consumed by inspect.\n    # See Issue #28233.\n    Tensor = torch.Tensor\n    ret: dict[Callable, Callable] = {\n        torch.abs: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.absolute: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.adaptive_avg_pool1d: lambda input, output_size: -1,\n        torch.adaptive_max_pool1d: lambda inputs, output_size: -1,\n        torch.acos: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.adjoint: lambda input: -1,\n        torch.arccos: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.acosh: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.arccosh: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.add: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.addbmm: lambda input, batch1, batch2, alpha=1, beta=1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.addcdiv: lambda input, tensor1, tensor2, value=1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.addcmul: lambda input, tensor1, tensor2, value=1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.addmm: lambda input, mat1, mat2, beta=1, alpha=1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.addmv: lambda input, mat, vec, beta=1, alpha=1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.addr: lambda input, vec1, vec2, beta=1, alpha=1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.affine_grid_generator: lambda theta, size, align_corners: -1,\n        torch.all: lambda input, dim=None: -1,\n        torch.allclose: lambda input, other, trol=1e-05, atol=1e-08, equal_nan=False: -1,\n        torch.alpha_dropout: lambda input, p, train, inplace=False: -1,\n        torch.amax: lambda input, dim=None: -1,\n        torch.amin: lambda input, dim=None: -1,\n        torch.aminmax: lambda input, dim=None, keepdim=False, out=None: -1,\n        torch.angle: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.any: lambda input, dim=None, keepdim=False, out=None: -1,\n        torch.argmax: lambda input: -1,\n        torch.argmin: lambda input: -1,\n        torch.argsort: lambda input, dim=None: -1,\n        torch.asin: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch._assert_async: lambda input, msg: -1,\n        torch.arcsin: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.asinh: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.arcsinh: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.atan: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.arctan: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.atan2: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.arctan2: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.atanh: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.arctanh: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.atleast_1d: lambda *tensors: -1,\n        torch.atleast_2d: lambda *tensors: -1,\n        torch.atleast_3d: lambda *tensors: -1,\n        torch.avg_pool1d: lambda input, kernel_size, stride=None, padding=0, ceil_mode=False, count_include_pad=True: -1,\n        torch.baddbmm: lambda input, batch1, batch2, alpha=1, beta=1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm: lambda input, weight, bias, running_mean, running_var, training, momentum, eps, cudnn_enabled: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm_backward_elemt: lambda grad_out, input, mean, invstd, weight, sum_dy, sum_dy_xmu, count_tensor: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm_backward_reduce: lambda grad_out, input, mean, invstd, weight, input_g, weight_g, bias_g: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm_elemt: lambda input, weight, bias, mean, invstd, eps: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm_gather_stats: lambda input, mean, invstd, running_mean, running_var, momentum, eps, count: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm_gather_stats_with_counts: lambda input, mean, invstd, running_mean, running_var, momentum, eps, count: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm_stats: lambda input, eps: -1,\n        torch.batch_norm_update_stats: lambda input, running_mean, running_var, momentum: -1,\n        torch.bernoulli: lambda input, generator=None, out=None: -1,\n        torch.bilinear: lambda input1, input2, weight, bias: -1,\n        torch.binary_cross_entropy_with_logits: (\n            lambda input, target, weight=None, size_average=None, reduce=None, reduction=\"mean\", pos_weight=None: -1\n        ),\n        torch.bincount: lambda input, weights=None, minlength=0: -1,\n        torch.binomial: lambda count, prob, generator=None: -1,\n        torch.bitwise_and: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.bitwise_not: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.bitwise_or: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.bitwise_xor: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.bitwise_left_shift: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.bitwise_right_shift: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.block_diag: lambda *tensors: -1,\n        torch.bmm: lambda input, mat2, out_dtype=None, out=None: -1,\n        torch.broadcast_tensors: lambda *tensors: -1,\n        torch.broadcast_to: lambda self, size: -1,\n        torch.bucketize: lambda input, boundaries, out_int32=False, right=False, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cartesian_prod: lambda *tensors: -1,\n        torch.cat: lambda tensors, dim=0, out=None: -1,\n        torch.concat: lambda tensors, dim=0, out=None: -1,  # alias for torch.cat\n        torch.concatenate: lambda tensors, dim=0, out=None: -1,  # alias for torch.concatenate\n        torch.cdist: lambda x1, x2, p=2.0, compute_mode=\"use_mm_for_euclid_dist_if_necessary\": -1,\n        torch.ceil: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.celu: lambda input, alpha=1.0, inplace=False: -1,\n        torch.chain_matmul: lambda *matrices, out=None: -1,\n        torch.channel_shuffle: lambda input, groups: -1,\n        torch.cholesky: lambda input, upper=False, out=None: -1,\n        torch.linalg.cholesky: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.linalg.cholesky_ex: lambda input, check_errors=False, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cholesky_inverse: lambda input, upper=False, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cholesky_solve: lambda input1, input2, upper=False, out=None: -1,\n        torch.choose_qparams_optimized: lambda input, numel, n_bins, ratio, bit_width: -1,\n        torch.chunk: lambda input, chunks, dim=0: -1,\n        torch.clamp: lambda input, min=None, max=None, out=None: -1,\n        torch.clip: lambda input, min=None, max=None, out=None: -1,\n        torch.clamp_min: lambda input, min, out=None: -1,\n        torch.clamp_max: lambda input, max, out=None: -1,\n        torch.column_stack: lambda tensors, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cov: lambda input, correction=1, fweights=None, aweights=None: -1,\n        torch.clone: lambda input: -1,\n        torch.combinations: lambda input, r=2, with_replacement=False: -1,\n        torch.complex: lambda real, imag: -1,\n        torch.copysign: lambda input, other, out=None: -1,\n        torch.polar: lambda abs, ang: -1,\n        torch.linalg.cond: lambda input, ord=None: -1,\n        torch.conj: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.conj_physical: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.resolve_conj: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.resolve_neg: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.constant_pad_nd: lambda input, pad, value=0: -1,\n        torch.conv1d: lambda input, weight, bias=None, stride=1, padding=0, dilation=1, groups=1: -1,\n        torch.conv2d: lambda input, weight, bias=None, stride=1, padding=0, dilation=1, groups=1: -1,\n        torch.conv3d: lambda input, weight, bias=None, stride=1, padding=0, dilation=1, groups=1: -1,\n        torch.convolution: lambda input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_adding, groups: -1,\n        torch.conv_tbc: lambda input, weight, bias, pad=0: -1,\n        torch.conv_transpose1d: lambda input, weight, bias=None, stride=1, padding=0, output_padding=0, groups=1, dilation=1: -1,\n        torch.conv_transpose2d: lambda input, weight, bias=None, stride=1, padding=0, output_padding=0, groups=1, dilation=1: -1,\n        torch.conv_transpose3d: lambda input, weight, bias=None, stride=1, padding=0, output_padding=0, groups=1, dilation=1: -1,\n        torch.corrcoef: lambda input: -1,\n        torch.cos: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cosine_embedding_loss: lambda input1, input2, target, margin=0, size_average=None, reduce=None, reduction=\"mean\": -1,\n        torch.cosh: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cosine_similarity: lambda x1, x2, dim=1, eps=1e-8: -1,\n        torch.count_nonzero: lambda input: -1,\n        torch.cross: lambda input, other, dim=None, out=None: -1,\n        torch.linalg.cross: lambda input, other, dim=-1, out=None: -1,\n        torch.ctc_loss: (\n            lambda log_probs, targets, input_lengths, target_lengths, blank=0, reduction=\"mean\", zero_infinity=False: -1\n        ),\n        torch.cummax: lambda input, dim, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cummin: lambda input, dim, out=None: -1,\n        torch.cumprod: lambda input, dim, out=None, dtype=None: -1,\n        torch.cumsum: lambda input, dim, out=None, dtype=None: -1,\n        torch.cumulative_trapezoid: lambda y, x=None, dim=-1: -1,\n        torch.logcumsumexp: lambda input, dim, out=None: -1,\n        torch.deg2rad: lambda input, out=None: -1,\n self, size1, size2, dense_dim: -1,\n        Tensor.sparse_resize_and_clear_: lambda self, size1, size2, dense_dim: -1,\n        Tensor.sspaddmm: lambda self, mat1, mat2, beta=1, alpha=1, out=None:
