Tensor.sum_to_size: lambda self, size: -1,\n        Tensor.tile: lambda self, *reps: -1,\n        Tensor.to: lambda self, dtype, non_blocking=False, copy=False, memory_format=torch.preserve_format: -1,\n        Tensor.to_dense: lambda self, dtype=None, *, masked_grad=None: -1,\n        Tensor._to_dense: lambda self, dtype=None, masked_grad=None: -1,\n        Tensor.to_sparse: lambda self: -1,\n        Tensor.tolist: lambda self: -1,\n        Tensor.to_mkldnn: lambda self: -1,\n        Tensor.type_as: lambda self, other: -1,\n        Tensor.unfold: lambda self, dimension, size, step: -1,\n        Tensor.uniform_: lambda self, from_=0, to=1: -1,\n        Tensor.values: lambda self: -1,\n        Tensor.view: lambda self, shape: -1,\n        Tensor.view_as: lambda self, other: -1,\n        Tensor.zero_: lambda self: -1,\n        Tensor.__dlpack__: lambda self, stream=None, max_version=None, dl_device=None, copy=None: -1,\n        Tensor.__dlpack_device__: lambda self: -1,\n        Tensor.index: lambda self, a, b: -1,\n        torch.linalg.lstsq: lambda self, b, cond=None, driver=None: -1,\n    }  # fmt: skip\n\n    privateuse1_backend_name = (\n        torch.utils.backend_registration._privateuse1_backend_name\n    )\n    if hasattr(Tensor, privateuse1_backend_name):\n        ret[getattr(Tensor, privateuse1_backend_name)] = (\n            lambda self, device=None, non_blocking=False, **kwargs: -1\n        )\n        ret[getattr(Tensor, f\"is_{privateuse1_backend_name}\").__get__] = lambda self: -1\n\n    ret2 = {}\n    ignored = get_ignored_functions()\n\n    for k, v in ret.items():\n        # Generate methods like __add__ and add_ by default from add\n        names = [\n            k.__name__,  # Default method\n            k.__name__ + \"_\",  # Inplace variant\n            \"__\" + k.__name__ + \"__\",  # Dunder method\n            \"__i\" + k.__name__ + \"__\",  # Inplace dunder method\n            \"__r\" + k.__name__ + \"__\",  # Reverse dunder method\n        ]\n\n        if k.__name__.startswith(\"bitwise_\"):\n            # bitwise_<op> have dunder methods of the form __<op>__\n            # And so on.\n            subname = k.__name__[len(\"bitwise_\") :]\n            names.extend(\n                [\"__\" + subname + \"__\", \"__i\" + subname + \"__\", \"__r\" + subname + \"__\"]\n            )\n\n        for name in names:\n            func = getattr(Tensor, name, None)\n            if callable(func) and func not in ret and func not in ignored:\n                ret2[func] = v\n\n    ret.update(ret2)\n    return ret\n\n\ndef wrap_torch_function(dispatcher: Callable):\n    \"\"\"Wraps a given function with ``__torch_function__`` -related functionality.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher: Callable\n        A callable that returns an iterable of Tensor-likes passed into the function.\n\n    Note\n    ----\n    This decorator may reduce the performance of your code. Generally, it's enough to express\n    your code as a series of functions that, themselves, support __torch_function__. If you\n    find yourself in the rare situation where this is not the case, e.g. if you're wrapping a\n    low-level library and you also need it to work for Tensor-likes, then this function is available.\n\n    Examples\n    --------\n    >>> def dispatcher(a):  # Must have the same signature as func\n    ...     return (a,)\n    >>> @torch.overrides.wrap_torch_function(dispatcher)\n    >>> def func(a):  # This will make func dispatchable by __torch_function__\n    ...     return a + 0\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def inner(func):\n        @functools.wraps(func)\n        def wrapped(*args, **kwargs):\n            relevant_args = dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)\n            if has_torch_function(relevant_args):\n                return handle_torch_function(wrapped, relevant_args, *args, **kwargs)\n\n            return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n        return wrapped\n\n    return inner\n\n\ndef _get_overloaded_args(\n    relevant_args: Iterable[Any],\n    get_type_fn: Callable[[Any], type] | None = None,\n) -> list[Any]:\n    \"\"\"Returns a list of arguments on which to call __torch_function__.\n\n    Checks arguments in relevant_args for __torch_function__ implementations,\n    storing references to the arguments and their types in overloaded_args and\n    overloaded_types in order of calling precedence. Only distinct types are\n    considered. If a type is a subclass of another type it will have higher\n    precedence, otherwise the precedence order is the same as the order of\n    arguments in relevant_args, that is, from left-to-right in the argument list.\n\n    The precedence-determining algorithm implemented in this function is\n    described in `NEP-0018`_.\n\n    See torch::append_overloaded_arg for the equivalent function in the C++\n    implementation.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    relevant_args : iterable of array-like\n        Iterable of array-like arguments to check for __torch_function__\n        methods.\n\n    get_type_fn : callable, optional\n        Function to call on each argument in relevant_args to get its type.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    overloaded_args : list\n        Arguments from relevant_args on which to call __torch_function__\n        methods, in the order in which they should be called.\n\n    .. _NEP-0018:\n       https://numpy.org/neps/nep-0018-array-function-protocol.html\n    \"\"\"\n    if get_type_fn is None:\n        get_type_fn = type\n\n    # If torch function is not enabled, there are no overloaded types\n    if not torch._C._is_torch_function_enabled():\n        return []\n    # Runtime is O(num_arguments * num_unique_types)\n    overloaded_types: set[type] = set()\n    overloaded_args: list[Any] = []\n    for arg in relevant_args:\n        arg_type = get_type_fn(arg)\n        # We only collect arguments if they have a unique type, which ensures\n        # reasonable performance even with a long list of possibly overloaded\n        # arguments.\n        #\n        # NB: Important to exclude _disabled_torch_function_impl, otherwise\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/64687\n        if (\n            arg_type not in overloaded_types\n            and hasattr(arg_type, \"__torch_function__\")\n            and arg_type.__torch_function__\n            is not torch._C._disabled_torch_function_impl\n        ):\n            # Create lists explicitly for the first type (usually the only one\n            # done) to avoid setting up the iterator for overloaded_args.\n            if overloaded_types:\n                overloaded_types.add(arg_type)\n                # By default, insert argument at the end, but if it is\n                # subclass of another argument, insert it before that argument.\n                # This ensures \"subclasses before superclasses\".\n                index = len(overloaded_args)\n                for i, old_arg in enumerate(overloaded_args):\n                    if issubclass(arg_type, get_type_fn(old_arg)):\n                        index = i\n                        break\n                overloaded_args.insert(index, arg)\n            else:\n                overloaded_types = {arg_type}\n                overloaded_args = [arg]\n    return overloaded_args\n\n\ndef handle_torch_function(\n    public_api: Callable,\n    relevant_args: Iterable[Any],\n    *args,\n    **kwargs,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Implement a function with checks for ``__torch_function__`` overrides.\n\n    See torch::autograd::handle_torch_function for the equivalent of this\n    function in the C++ implementation.\n\n    Arguments\n    ---------\n    public_api : function\n        Function exposed by the public torch API originally called like\n        ``public_api(*args, **kwargs)`` on which arguments are now being\n        checked.\n    relevant_args : iterable\n        Iterable of arguments to check for __torch_function__ methods.\n    args : tuple\n        Arbitrary positional arguments originally passed into ``public_api``.\n    kwargs : tuple\n        Arbitrary keyword arguments originally passed into ``public_api``.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    object\n        Result from calling ``implementation`` or an ``__torch_function__``\n        method, as appropriate.\n\n    Raises\n    ------\n    TypeError : if no implementation is found.\n\n    Example\n    -------\n    >>> def func(a):\n    ...     if has_torch_function_unary(a):\n    ...         return handle_torch_function(func, (a,), a)\n    ...     return a + 0\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check for __torch_function__ methods.\n    overloaded_args = _get_overloaded_args(relevant_args)\n    # overloaded_args already have unique types.\n    types = tuple(map(type, overloaded_args))\n\n    # Check for __torch_function__ mode.\n    if _is_torch_function_mode_enabled():\n        # if we're here, the mode must be set to a TorchFunctionStackMode\n        # this unsets it and calls directly into TorchFunctionStackMode's torch function\n        with _pop_mode_temporarily() as mode:\n            result = mode.__torch_function__(public_api, types, args, kwargs)\n        if result is not NotImplemented:\n            return result\n\n    # Call overrides\n    for overloaded_arg in overloaded_args:\n        # This call needs to become a classmethod call in the future.\n        # See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/63767\n        torch_func_method = overloaded_arg.__torch_function__\n        if (\n            hasattr(torch_func_method, \"__self__\")\n            and torch_func_method.__self__ is overloaded_arg\n            and torch_func_method is not torch._C._disabled_torch_function_impl\n        ):\n            warnings.warn(\n                \"Defining your `__torch_function__ as a plain method is deprecated and \"\n                \"will be an error in future, please define it as a classmethod.\",\n                DeprecationWarning,\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n\n        # Use `public_api` instead of `implementation` so __torch_function__\n        # implementations can do equality/identity comparisons.\n        result = torch_func_method(public_api, types, args, kwargs)\n\n        if result is not NotImplemented:\n            return result\n\n    func_name = f\"{public_api.__module__}.{public_api.__name__}\"\n    msg = (\n        f\"no implementation found for '{func_name}' on types that implement \"\n        f\"__torch_function__: {[type(arg) for arg in overloaded_args]}\"\n    )\n    if _is_torch_function_mode_enabled():\n        msg += f\" nor in mode {_get_current_function_mode()}\"\n    raise TypeError(msg)\n\n\nhas_torch_function = _add_docstr(\n    _has_torch_function,\n    r\"\"\"Check for __torch_function__ implementations in the elements of an iterable\n    or if a __torch_function__ mode is enabled.  Considers exact ``Tensor`` s\n    and ``Parameter`` s non-dispatchable.  Use this to guard a call to\n    :func:`handle_torch_function`; don't use it to test if something\n    is Tensor-like, use :func:`is_tensor_like` instead.\n    Arguments\n    ---------\n    relevant_args : iterable\n        Iterable or arguments to check for __torch_function__ methods.\n    Returns\n    -------\n    bool\n        True if any of the elements of relevant_args have __torch_function__\n        implementations, False otherwise.\n    See Also\n    ________\n    torch.is_tensor_like\n        Checks if something is a Tensor-like, including an exact ``Tensor``.\n    \"\"\",\n)\n\nhas_torch_function_unary = _add_docstr(\n    _has_torch_function_unary,\n    r\"\"\"Special case of `has_torch_function` for single inputs.\n    Instead of:\n      `has_torch_function((t,))`\n    call:\n      `has_torch_function_unary(t)`\n    which skips unnecessary packing and unpacking work.\n    \"\"\",\n)\n\nhas_torch_function_variadic = _add_docstr(\n    _has_torch_function_variadic,\n    r\"\"\"Special case of `has_torch_function` that skips tuple creation.\n\n    This uses the METH_FASTCALL protocol introduced in Python 3.7\n\n    Instead of:\n      `has_torch_function((a, b))`\n    call:\n      `has_torch_function_variadic(a, b)`\n    which skips unnecessary packing and unpacking work.\n    \"\"\",\n)\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _get_overridable_functions() -> tuple[\n    dict[Any, list[Callable]], dict[Callable, str]\n]:\n    overridable_funcs = collections.defaultdict(list)\n    index = {}\n    tested_namespaces = [\n        (\"torch\", torch, torch.__all__),\n        (\"torch.functional\", torch.functional, torch.functional.__all__),\n        (\"torch.nn.functional\", torch.nn.functional, dir(torch.nn.functional)),\n        (\"torch.nn.init\", torch.nn.init, dir(torch.nn.init)),\n        (\"torch.Tensor\", torch.Tensor, dir(torch.Tensor)),\n        (\"torch.linalg\", torch.linalg, dir(torch.linalg)),\n        (\"torch.fft\", torch.fft, dir(torch.fft)),\n        (\"torch.special\", torch.special, dir(torch.special)),\n    ]\n    for namespace_str, namespace, ns_funcs in tested_namespaces:\n        for func_name in ns_funcs:\n            ignore = False\n            # ignore private functions or functions that are deleted in torch.__init__\n            if namespace is not torch.Tensor:\n                if func_name.startswith(\"__\"):\n                    continue\n                elif func_name.startswith(\"_\"):\n                    ignore = True\n                elif func_name.endswith(\"_\"):\n                    ignore = True\n                elif not func_name[0].islower():\n                    ignore = True\n                elif func_name == \"unique_dim\":\n                    continue\n            else:\n                func = getattr(namespace, func_name)\n                if getattr(object, func_name, None) == func:\n                    continue\n                if func_name == \"__weakref__\":\n                    continue\n            func = getattr(namespace, func_name)\n            if namespace is torch.Tensor and getattr(object, func_name, None) == func:\n                continue\n            # ignore re-exported modules\n            if isinstance(func, types.ModuleType):\n                continue\n            # ignore __future__ imports\n            if isinstance(func, __future__._Feature):\n                continue\n\n            if not callable(func) and hasattr(func, \"__get__\"):\n                index[func.__get__] = f\"{namespace_str}.{func_name}.__get__\"\n                index[func.__set__] = f\"{namespace_str}.{func_name}.__set__\"\n                if ignore:\n                    continue\n                if func.__get__ in get_ignored_functions():\n                    msg = (\n                        \"{}.{} is in the tuple returned by torch._overrides.get_ignored_functions \"\n                        \"but still has an explicit override\"\n                    )\n                    assert func.__get__ not in get_testing_overrides(), msg.format(\n                        namespace, func.__name__\n                    )\n                    continue\n                else:\n                    overridable_funcs[func].append(func.__get__)\n                    continue\n\n            if not callable(func):\n                continue\n\n            index[func] = f\"{namespace_str}.{func_name}\"\n\n            if ignore:\n                continue\n\n            # cannot be overridden by __torch_function__\n            if func in get_ignored_functions():\n                msg = (\n                    \"{}.{} is in the tuple returned by torch._overrides.get_ignored_functions \"\n                    \"but still has an explicit override\"\n                )\n                assert func not in get_testing_overrides(), msg.format(\n                    namespace, func.__name__\n                )\n                continue\n            overridable_funcs[namespace].append(func)\n    return overridable_funcs, index\n\n\n@_disable_user_warnings\ndef get_overridable_functions() -> dict[Any, list[Callable]]:\n    \"\"\"List functions that are overridable via __torch_function__\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Dict[Any, List[Callable]]\n        A dictionary that maps namespaces that contain overridable functions\n        to functions in that namespace that can be overridden.\n    \"\"\"\n    return _get_overridable_functions()[0]\n\n\n@_disable_user_warnings\ndef resolve_name(f):\n    \"\"\"Get a human readable string name for a function passed to\n    __torch_function__\n\n    Arguments\n    ---------\n    f : Callable\n        Function to resolve the name of.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    str\n        Name of the function; if eval'ed it should give back the input\n        function.\n    \"\"\"\n    if isinstance(f, (torch._ops.OpOverload, torch._ops.OpOverloadPacket)):\n        return str(f)\n    return _get_overridable_functions()[1].get(f)\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _get_tensor_methods() -> set[Callable]:\n    \"\"\"Returns a set of the overridable methods on ``torch.Tensor``\"\"\"\n    overridable_funcs = get_overridable_functions()\n    methods = set(overridable_funcs[torch.Tensor])\n    return methods\n\n\n@_disable_user_warnings\ndef is_tensor_method_or_property(func: Callable) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns True if the function passed in is a handler for a\n    method or property belonging to ``torch.Tensor``, as passed\n    into ``__torch_function__``.\n\n    .. note::\n       For properties, their ``__get__`` method must be passed in.\n\n    This may be needed, in particular, for the following reasons:\n\n    1. Methods/properties sometimes don't contain a `__module__` slot.\n    2. They require that the first passed-in argument is an instance\n       of ``torch.Tensor``.\n\n    Examples\n    --------\n    >>> is_tensor_method_or_property(torch.Tensor.add)\n    True\n    >>> is_tensor_method_or_property(torch.add)\n    False\n    \"\"\"\n    return func in _get_tensor_methods() or func.__name__ == \"__get__\"\n\n\ndef is_tensor_like(inp):\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns ``True`` if the passed-in input is a Tensor-like.\n\n    Currently, this occurs whenever there's a ``__torch_function__``\n    attribute on the type of the input.\n\n    Examples\n    --------\n    A subclass of tensor is generally a Tensor-like.\n\n    >>> class SubTensor(torch.Tensor): ...\n    >>> is_tensor_like(SubTensor([0]))\n    True\n\n    Built-in or user types aren't usually Tensor-like.\n\n    >>> is_tensor_like(6)\n    False\n    >>> is_tensor_like(None)\n    False\n    >>> class NotATensor: ...\n    >>> is_tensor_like(NotATensor())\n    False\n\n    But, they can be made Tensor-like by implementing __torch_function__.\n\n    >>> class TensorLike:\n    ...     @classmethod\n    ...     def __torch_function__(cls, func, types, args, kwargs):\n    ...         return -1\n    >>> is_tensor_like(TensorLike())\n    True\n    \"\"\"\n    return type(inp) is torch.Tensor or hasattr(inp, \"__torch_function__\")\n\n\nclass TorchFunctionMode:\n    \"\"\"\n    A ``TorchFunctionMode`` allows you to override the meaning of all\n    ``__torch_function__`` overridable functions within a dynamic scope,\n    without having to actually create a tensor subclass or manually\n    monkey-patch functions in the PyTorch API.  Some common situations\n    where you should use a mode:\n\n        * You want to override the meaning of factory functions, or other\n          functions that do not otherwise take a tensor as an argument\n          (these cannot be overridden with tensor subclasses).\n\n        * You want to override the behavior of all functions without needing\n          to wrap your inputs in tensor subclasses; e.g., if you are just\n          interested in logging intermediate computations.\n\n        * You want to control the order of execution of various tensor\n          subclasses explicitly, rather than implicitly via the return of\n          ``NotImplemented``.\n\n    Independent subclasses of :class:`TorchFunctionMode` are compositional:\n    modes can be pushed onto a stack using ``with MyMode():``.\n    When you call functions in the PyTorch API inside your\n    ``__torch_function__`` implementation, by default, they will forward on to\n    the next mode on the mode stack.  If you want recursively call back into\n    your current ``__torch_function__`` implementation, either explicitly\n    invoke ``self.__torch_function__(...)``, or use the context manager\n    ``enable_torch_function_mode(self, replace=self.inner)`` to make PyTorch\n    API self-referential (beware of infinite loops, in this case!)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    inner: \"TorchFunctionMode\"\n\n    # Force metaclass to generate constructor at the base of the hierarchy\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        pass\n\n    def __torch_function__(self, func, types, args=(), kwargs=None):\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def __enter__(self):\n        _push_mode(self)\n        return self\n\n    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):\n        _pop_mode()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def push(cls, *args, **kwargs):\n        warnings.warn(\n            \"`Mode.push()` is no longer necessary and can be replaced with just `with Mode()`\",\n            stacklevel=2,\n        )\n        instance = cls(*args, **kwargs)\n        return instance\n\n\ndef _get_current_function_mode():\n    stack_len = _len_torch_function_stack()\n    return _get_function_stack_at(stack_len - 1) if stack_len > 0 else None\n\n\ndef _get_current_function_mode_stack():\n    stack_len = _len_torch_function_stack()\n    return [_get_function_stack_at(i) for i in range(stack_len)]\n\n\ndef _push_mode(mode):\n    _push_on_torch_function_stack(mode)\n\n\ndef _pop_mode():\n    old = _pop_torch_function_stack()\n    return old\n\n\n@contextlib.contextmanager\ndef _pop_mode_temporarily():\n    old = _pop_mode()\n    try:\n        yield old\n    finally:\n        _push_mode(old)\n\n\nclass BaseTorchFunctionMode(TorchFunctionMode):\n    def __torch_function__(self, func, types, args=(), kwargs=None):\n        if kwargs is None:\n            kwargs = {}\n        return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n\n@contextlib.contextmanager\ndef _enable_torch_function():\n    old_state = torch._C._get_torch_function_state()\n    try:\n        torch._C._set_torch_function_state(torch._C._TorchFunctionState.ENABLED)\n        yield\n    finally:\n        torch._C._set_torch_function_state(old_state)\n\n\n@contextlib.contextmanager\ndef enable_reentrant_dispatch():\n    # NB: this can't simply be\n    # `enable_reentrant_dispatch = torch._C._RestorePythonTLSSnapshot`\n    # because:\n    # 1. torch._C._RestorePythonTLSSnapshot is unavailable when this file\n    #    initially gets imported. Probably an import order thing.\n    # 2. enable_reentrant_dispatch is technically public API; assigning\n    #    it the object would change the __module__ to look private.\n    with torch._C._RestorePythonTLSSnapshot():\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            pass\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/trace_rules.py",
  "newString": "        \"torch._add_relu_\",\n        \"torch._add_relu\",\n        \"torch._addmm_activation\",\n        \"torch._aminmax\",",
  "oldString": "        \"torch._add_relu_\",\n        \"torch._add_relu\",\n        \"torch._aminmax\",",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nTracing rules and policies for TorchDynamo compilation decisions.\n\nThis module defines the rules that govern what code TorchDynamo should trace and compile\nversus what should be executed eagerly. It contains functions and classes that determine:\n\n- Which modules, functions, and objects should be skipped during tracing\n- Which parts of the code should cause graph breaks\n- How to handle different Python libraries and third-party packages\n- Rules for determining when to inline functions vs calling them eagerly\n\nKey components:\n- Skip rules: Functions that return True if an object should be skipped during tracing\n- Inlining rules: Policies for when to inline function calls during compilation\n- Library-specific handling: Special cases for popular Python packages\n- Performance heuristics: Rules that balance compilation overhead vs runtime benefits\n\nThese rules are critical for TorchDynamo's ability to automatically determine\ncompilation boundaries and optimize PyTorch programs effectively.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport abc\nimport builtins\nimport copy\nimport dataclasses\nimport functools\nimport importlib\nimport inspect\nimport linecache\nimport operator\nimport os\nimport random\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport traceback\nimport types\nimport unittest\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom collections.abc import Callable\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._inductor.test_operators\nimport torch.distributed\nimport torch.utils._content_store\nfrom torch._environment import is_fbcode\nfrom torch.utils import _config_module\n\nfrom . import config\nfrom .resume_execution import TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX\nfrom .utils import (\n    getfile,\n    hashable,\n    is_lru_cache_wrapped_function,\n    NP_SUPPORTED_MODULES,\n    unwrap_if_wrapper,\n)\nfrom .variables import (\n    BuiltinVariable,\n    FunctionalCallVariable,\n    FunctorchHigherOrderVariable,\n    LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n    LocalGeneratorObjectVariable,\n    NestedUserFunctionVariable,\n    PolyfilledFunctionVariable,\n    PyTreeGetNodeTypeFunctionVariable,\n    PyTreeTreeIsLeafFunctionVariable,\n    ReparametrizeModuleCallVariable,\n    SkipFunctionVariable,\n    TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    UserFunctionVariable,\n    UserMethodVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.base import VariableTracker\n\n\nnp: Optional[types.ModuleType] = None\ntry:\n    import numpy as np\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:\n    pass\n\n\n\"\"\"\nA note on skip/inline rules:\n\nDynamo consults this file to determine whether function should be inlined or skipped.\n\nA skip applies at the frame boundary, meaning dynamo either triggers a graph break\nat the beginning of the frame or attempts to trace/inline the whole frame. When skipping\na frame, recursively called frames are still traced by dynamo unless also skipped.\n\nSkipfiles (skipped at the file level instead of function level) still apply on a\nframe-by-frame boundary as dynamo traces, but apply to all functions in that file.\n\n@skip is a helper decorator that can be applied to your function to cause it to be\nincluded here.\n\nDynamo skip/inline rules & priorities are defined as follows:\n* Inline is the default behavior and will be used unless explicitly skipped.\n* Dynamo has two SKIPLIST: BUILTIN_SKIPLIST and THIRDPARTY_SKIPLIST.\n    * BUILTIN_SKIPLIST contains builtin python modules, such as abc, collections, etc.\n    * THIRDPARTY_SKIPLIST contains common third party libraries, such as numpy, pandas, etc.\n* Functions in these two SKIPLISTs are always skipped, except:\n    * They have explicitly defined rule in `manual_torch_name_rule_map`;\n    * The corresponding python module has been put into MOD_INLINELIST.\n* PyTorch(torch) is in the BUILTIN_SKIPLIST by default, but there are many cases\n    where we want inline the functions under torch namespace.\n    We should specify inline for the functions in `manual_torch_name_rule_map` or\n    put the corresponding python module into MOD_INLINELIST to make dynamo inline them.\n* If you call functions under skipped modules/files, Dynamo will wrap these functions\n    as SkipFunctionVariable. There are a few functions(e.g, collections.OrderedDict) that\n    we have special handling at SkipFunctionVariable.call_function.\n\nOverall: *_INLINELIST has precedence over *_SKIPLIST has precedence over DEFAULT (inline)\n\nTo figure out what the behavior is, check the following list in order:\n* `manual_torch_name_rule_map` (Inline if YES)\n* MOD_INLINELIST (Inline if YES)\n* BUILTIN_SKIPLIST & THIRDPARTY_SKIPLIST (Skip if YES)\n* MOD_SKIPLIST (Skip if YES)\n* Inline by default\n\nIn general, if you want to force inline a function or module, please consider adding\nthe function's python module to MOD_INLINELIST first.\nUse the `manual_torch_name_rule_map` only when there are other functions under the same module that\nyou don't want to inline them.\n\"\"\"\n\n\"\"\"\nMap of function objects to their tracing rules (Dynamo variables).\n* TorchInGraphFunctionVariable: The functions should be put into the FX graph or can be constant folded. E.g.,\n  - torch.add: should be put into the FX graph.\n  - torch.is_floating_point: constant folded.\n* SkipFunctionVariable: The objects should be skipped from tracing.\n* UserFunctionVariable: The functions should be inlined.\n\nFor developers: If you add/remove a torch level API, it may trigger failures from\ntest/dynamo/test_trace_rules.py:test_torch_name_rule_map_updated. To fix the failures:\nIf you are adding a new torch level API or Dynamo implementation:\n* Add the name with the corresponding tracing rule to this map\n  if you are adding a new in graph function or Dynamo implementation for an existing function.\n* Remove the object name from test/dynamo/test_trace_rules.ignored_c_binding_in_graph_function_names if it's there.\n\nIf you are removing an existing torch level API:\n* Remove the entry represented the API from this map or test/dynamo/test_trace_rules.ignored_c_binding_in_graph_function_names\n  depends on where it is.\n\n\n\"\"\"\nmanual_torch_name_rule_map: dict[\n    str,\n    Union[\n        type[TorchInGraphFunctionVariable],\n        type[SkipFunctionVariable],\n        type[UserFunctionVariable],\n    ],\n] = {\n    \"torch.onnx.is_in_onnx_export\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.onnx.operators.shape_as_tensor\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.overrides.is_tensor_like\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.jit.is_scripting\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.jit.is_tracing\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.jit.annotate\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.is_available\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.is_initialized\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.get_rank\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.get_world_size\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.tensor._api.DTensor#from_local\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.distributed_c10d._get_group_size_by_name\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.distributed_c10d._resolve_group_name_by_ranks_and_tag\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.distributed_c10d._get_group_tag\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.distributed.distributed_c10d.get_process_group_ranks\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch._utils.is_compiling\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.fx._symbolic_trace.is_fx_tracing\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.fx._symbolic_trace.is_fx_symbolic_tracing\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch._dynamo.external_utils.is_compiling\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer\": UserFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.compiler.is_compiling\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.compiler.is_exporting\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch._C._to_dlpack\": SkipFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch.to_dlpack\": SkipFunctionVariable,\n    \"torch._check\": TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n    # We graph break on RNG state setters or getters like\n    # `torch.get_rng_state` or `torch.set_rng_state`. These functions\n    # are not aten operations and therefore they are completely ignored\n    # by the AOT dispatcher. As a result, the AOT graph does not have\n    # these setter or getter functions, producing an incorrect graph\n    # when it comes to rng states.\n    \"torch.default_generator#get_state\": torch_c_binding_in_graph_functions[\"math.cbrt\"] = TorchInGraphFunctionVariable\n\nif sys.version_info >= (3, 13):\n    torch_c_binding_in_graph_functions[\"math.fma\"] = TorchInGraphFunctionVariable\n\n# In graph functions (including constant folding) that are not C bindings\n# NOTE: [Cacheability of in-graph torch functions]\n# Functions in this list have the property that graphs containing them are safe to cache/serialize.\n# serialize given only the information in the graph. I.e, either:\n# - Your function does not access or close over global state, or\n# - Your function closes over global state, but this state is guarded by dynamo, either\n#   through constant folding or other mechanisms\n# If your function needs a custom special handler (via @register on TorchInGraphFunctionVariable),\n# or captures global state, please add it to manual_torch_name_rule_map instead\ntorch_non_c_binding_in_graph_functions = dict.fromkeys(\n    [\n        \"torch.__future__.get_overwrite_module_params_on_conversion\",\n      \"torch.xpu.get_arch_list\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_device_capability\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_device_name\",\n        \"torch.xpu.get_device_properties\",\n \"torch.xpu.memory.mem_get_info\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_allocated\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_reserved\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_stats_as_nested_dict\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.memory_stats\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.reset_accumulated_memory_stats\",\n        \"torch.xpu.memory.reset_peak_memory_stats\",\n        \"torch.xpu.random.initial_seed\",\n        \"torch.xpu.random.seed_all\",\n        \"torch.xpu.random.seed\",\n        \"torch.xpu.set_stream\",\n        \"torch.xpu.stream\",\n        \"torch.xpu.synchronize\",\n    ],\n    TorchInGraphFunctionVariable,\n)\n\n\ntorch_name_rule_map = [\n    manual_torch_name_rule_map,\n    torch_c_binding_in_graph_functions,\n    torch_non_c_binding_in_graph_functions,\n]\n\n\n\"\"\"\nGenerate the torch object - Dynamo tracing rule (the wrapping variable) map.\n\"\"\"\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_torch_obj_rule_map() -> dict[Any, type[\"VariableTracker\"]]:\n    d: dict[Any, type[VariableTracker]] = {}\n    for m in torch_name_rule_map:\n        for k, v in m.items():  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            if \".py#\" not in k:\n                obj = load_object(k)\n            else:\n                torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n                if torch_dir is None:\n                    continue\n                obj = torch_dir + k[len(\"torch/\") :]\n            if obj is not None:\n                if is_lru_cache_wrapped_function(obj):\n                    obj = obj.__wrapped__\n                if obj in d and d[obj] != v:\n                    raise AssertionError(\n                        f\"Duplicate torch object {obj} with different rules: {v}, {d[obj]}\"\n                    )\n                else:\n                    d[obj] = v\n    return d\n\n\ndef _load_obj_from_str(fully_qualified_name: str) -> Any:\n    module, obj_name = fully_qualified_name.rsplit(\".\", maxsplit=1)\n    return getattr(importlib.import_module(module), obj_name)\n\n\n\"\"\"\nLoad string represented torch objects.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_object(name: str) -> Any:\n    try:\n        x = name.split(\"#\")\n        if len(x) == 2:\n            obj = _load_obj_from_str(x[0])\n            val = getattr(obj, x[1])\n        else:\n            assert len(x) == 1, f\"Invalid obj name {name}\"\n            val = _load_obj_from_str(x[0])\n        val = unwrap_if_wrapper(val)\n    except (AttributeError, ImportError):\n        val = None\n    return val\n\n\n\"\"\"\nGet all torch.Tensor methods which are allowed to be in graph functions.\n\"\"\"\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_tensor_method() -> frozenset[Any]:\n    disallowed_tensor_methods = {\"__new__\", \"_make_wrapper_subclass\", \"_make_subclass\"}\n    s = set()\n    for name in dir(torch.Tensor):\n        method = getattr(torch.Tensor, name)\n        if (\n            isinstance(\n                method,\n                (\n                    types.MethodDescriptorType,\n                    types.WrapperDescriptorType,\n                    types.BuiltinFunctionType,\n                ),\n            )\n            and name not in disallowed_tensor_methods\n        ):\n            s.add(method)\n\n    # mlazos: these are functions which we handle specially in TensorVariable\n    s.add(torch.Tensor.__contains__)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n    s.add(torch.Tensor.register_hook)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n    return frozenset(s)\n\n\n\"\"\"\nReturn if a torch object is ATen op or torch.Tensor method.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef is_aten_op_or_tensor_method(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    return obj in get_tensor_method() or isinstance(\n        obj,\n        (torch._ops.OpOverloadPacket, torch._ops.OpOverload),\n    )\n\n\nclass FunctionIdSet:\n    \"\"\"\n    Track a set of `id()`s of objects which are either allowed or not\n    allowed to go into the generated FX graph.  Use to test for torch.*,\n    numpy.*, builtins.*, etc.\n\n    Support user modification to permit customization of what can be\n    added to the graph and what will cause a graph break.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    function_ids: Optional[set[int]] = None\n    function_names: Optional[dict[int, str]] = None\n\n    def __init__(\n        self, lazy_initializer: Callable[[], Union[dict[int, str], set[int]]]\n    ) -> None:\n        self.lazy_initializer = lazy_initializer\n\n    def __call__(self) -> set[int]:\n        if self.function_ids is None:\n            value = self.lazy_initializer()\n            if isinstance(value, dict):\n                self.function_ids = set(value.keys())\n                self.function_names = value\n            else:\n                assert isinstance(value, set)\n                self.function_ids = value\n        return self.function_ids\n\n    def get_name(self, idx: int, default: str) -> str:\n        self()  # lazy init\n        assert self.function_names is not None\n        return self.function_names.get(idx, default)\n\n    def add(self, idx: int) -> None:\n        function_ids = self()  # lazy init\n        function_ids.add(idx)\n\n    def remove(self, idx: int) -> None:\n        function_ids = self()\n        if idx in function_ids:\n            function_ids.remove(idx)\n\n    def __contains__(self, idx: int) -> bool:\n        return idx in self()\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _allowed_callable_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    rv: dict[int, str] = {}\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _disallowed_callable_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    rv: dict[int, str] = {}\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _nonstrict_trace_callable_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    rv: dict[int, str] = {}\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _builtin_function_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    # See also torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py, which removes items in _builtin_function_ids\n    rv = {\n        id(v): f\"builtins.{k}\"\n        for k, v in builtins.__dict__.items()\n        if not k.startswith(\"_\") and callable(v)\n    }\n    rv.update(\n        {\n            id(v): f\"operator.{k}\"\n            for k, v in operator.__dict__.items()\n            if not k.startswith(\"_\") and callable(v)\n        }\n    )\n    rv.update(\n        {\n            id(cast): \"typing.cast\",\n            id(copy.deepcopy): \"copy.deepcopy\",\n        }\n    )\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _polyfilled_function_ids() -> set[int]:\n    # See also @torch._dynamo.decorators.substitute_in_graph(...), which adds items in _polyfilled_function_ids\n    return set()\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _numpy_function_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    unsupported_funcs = {\n        \"seed\",\n        \"ranf\",\n        \"get_bit_generator\",\n        \"RandomState\",\n        \"set_bit_generator\",\n        \"sample\",\n    }\n\n    def is_supported(k: str, v: Any, mod: Any) -> bool:\n        if not callable(v):\n            return False\n        if not getattr(v, \"__module__\", None):\n            return True\n        if v.__module__ == mod.__name__:\n            return True\n        if (\n            v.__module__ == \"numpy.random.mtrand\"\n            and mod.__name__ == \"numpy.random\"\n            and k not in unsupported_funcs\n        ):\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    rv = {}\n    for mod in NP_SUPPORTED_MODULES:\n        for k, v in mod.__dict__.items():\n            if is_supported(k, v, mod):\n                rv[id(v)] = f\"{mod.__name__}.{k}\"\n    return rv\n\n\n@FunctionIdSet\ndef _builtin_constant_ids() -> dict[int, str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Collects constant builtins by eliminating callable items.\n    \"\"\"\n    rv = {\n        id(v): f\"builtins.{k}\"\n        for k, v in builtins.__dict__.items()\n        if not k.startswith(\"_\") and not callable(v)\n    }\n    return rv\n\n\n_lazy_module_init: dict[str, list[Callable[[], None]]] = defaultdict(list)\n\n\ndef add_module_init_func(name: str, init_func: Callable[[], None]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Register a module without eagerly importing it\"\"\"\n    # If the module is already imported, eagerly run init\n    assert \".\" not in name, f\"Expected a root module name, but got {name}\"\n    assert name not in _lazy_module_init\n    _lazy_module_init[name].append(init_func)\n\n\ndef _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj: object) -> None:\n    module = getattr(obj, \"__module__\", None)\n    if module is None:\n        return\n\n    base_module = module.split(\".\")[0]\n    init_funcs = _lazy_module_init.pop(base_module, None)\n    if init_funcs is not None:\n        for fn in init_funcs:\n            fn()\n\n\ndef is_callable_allowed(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return id(obj) in _allowed_callable_ids\n\n\ndef is_nonstrict_trace_callable(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return id(obj) in _nonstrict_trace_callable_ids\n\n\ndef is_callable_disallowed(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return id(obj) in _disallowed_callable_ids\n\n\ndef is_forbidden(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    _maybe_init_lazy_module(obj)\n    return inspect.getattr_static(obj, \"_dynamo_forbidden\", False)\n\n\ndef is_builtin_callable(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    # See also torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py, which removes items in _builtin_function_ids\n    return id(obj) in _builtin_function_ids\n\n\ndef is_builtin_constant(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    return id(obj) in _builtin_constant_ids\n\n\ndef is_polyfilled_callable(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    # See also @torch._dynamo.decorators.substitute_in_graph(...), which adds items in _polyfilled_function_ids\n    return id(obj) in _polyfilled_function_ids\n\n\ndef is_numpy(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    if np is None:\n        return False\n    return isinstance(obj, (np.ndarray, np.generic)) or id(obj) in _numpy_function_ids\n\n\ndef is_numpy_dtype(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    if np is None:\n        return False\n    return isinstance(obj, np.dtype)\n\n\ndef is_numpy_type_info(obj: Any) -> bool:\n    if np is None:\n        return False\n    return isinstance(obj, (np.finfo, np.iinfo))\n\n\nBUILTIN_SKIPLIST = (\n    abc,\n    copy,\n    random,\n    traceback,\n    linecache,\n)\n\n# third party libraries skiplist is defined by str, because users may not use these libraries.\n# we should use lazy import & skip in the future.\nTHIRDPARTY_SKIPLIST = (\n    \"fx2trt_oss\",\n    \"hypothesis\",\n    \"networkx\",\n    \"numpy\",\n    \"onnx\",\n    \"onnxruntime\",\n    \"onnx_tf\",\n    \"pandas\",\n    \"sklearn\",\n    \"tabulate\",\n    \"tensorflow\",\n    \"tensorrt\",\n    \"torch2trt\",\n    \"tqdm\",\n    \"tree\",\n    \"tvm\",\n    \"xarray\",\n)\n\n\ndef _as_posix_path(path: str) -> str:\n    posix_path = Path(os.path.normpath(path)).as_posix()\n    # os.path.normpath and pathlib.Path remove trailing slash, so we need to add it back\n    if path.endswith((os.path.sep, \"/\")):\n        posix_path += \"/\"\n    return posix_path\n\n\ndef _strip_init_py(s: str) -> str:\n    suffix = \"__init__.py\"\n    s = s.removesuffix(suffix)\n    return _as_posix_path(s)\n\n\ndef _module_dir(m: types.ModuleType) -> Optional[str]:\n    # Protect against a module not exporting __file__ - this can happen for\n    # frozen modules, for example.\n    file = getattr(m, \"__file__\", None)\n    return file and _strip_init_py(file)\n\n\n# These are legacy workarounds, don't add new modules to this list.\n# Please use the MOD_INLINELIST instead to force inline functions under particular modules.\n#\n# NB: The only thing that is different about MOD_INLINELIST and LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST\n# is the behavior of a function f2 in the module when called by a function f1\n# in a module in MOD_SKIPLIST (see MOD_SKIPLIST for more details)\n#\n# LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST is the same thing as Dynamo's behavior on a module that\n# is not in any *_INLINELIST or *_SKIPLIST.\n# That being said, we prefer people to add things to MOD_INLINELIST over\n# LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST because it is less likely to break existing tests.\nLEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST = {\n    \"torch._dynamo.external_utils\",\n    \"torch._export.db.examples\",\n    \"torch._export.wrappers\",\n    \"torch._functorch.apis\",\n    \"torch._functorch.deprecated\",\n    \"torch.nn.attention.flex_attention\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.stubs\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.export_utils\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.qat_utils\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.representation.rewrite\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.pt2e.utils\",\n    \"torch.ao.quantization.quantizer.xnnpack_quantizer\",\n    \"torch.export.unflatten\",\n}\n\nif torch.distributed.is_available():\n    LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST |= {\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor._api\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.device_mesh\",\n        \"torch.distributed.device_mesh\",\n        \"torch.distributed.algorithms._checkpoint.checkpoint_wrapper\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.parallel._data_parallel_utils\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.parallel._utils\",\n        \"torch.distributed.tensor.parallel.style\",\n        # we have to add replicate to LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST to ensure\n        # the forward_hook won't be ignored.\n        \"torch.distributed._composable.replicate\",\n    }\n    if not config.skip_fsdp_hooks:\n        LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST.add(\"torch.distributed.fsdp._fully_shard\")\n\n# Force inline functions under these modules, even they are in *_SKIPLIST.\n# We are using python module name instead of file or directory object to avoid circular dependency.\n# Please keep this sorted alphabetically.\n#\n# Btw, it is not \"ideal\" for something to be in MOD_INLINELIST. If Dynamo\n# fully supports a module, then the ideal case is that it is not in\n# any *_INLINELIST or *_SKIPLIST: then, the behavior of Dynamo is that\n# it will always inline into functions in the module.\nMOD_INLINELIST = [\n    \"torch._decomp\",\n    \"torch._dynamo._trace_wrapped_higher_order_op\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.comptime\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.polyfills\",\n    \"torch._dynamo.test_case\",\n    \"torch._export.non_strict_utils\",\n    \"torch._functorch._aot_autograd.subclass_parametrization\",\n    \"torch._functorch.autograd_function\",\n    \"torch._functorch.eager_transforms\",\n    \"torch._functorch.functional_call\",\n    \"torch._functorch.pyfunctorch\",\n    \"torch._functorch.vmap\",\n    \"torch._inductor.test_operators\",\n    \"torch._library.autograd\",\n    \"torch._library.custom_ops\",\n    \"torch._ops\",\n    \"torch._prims\",\n    \"torch._refs\",\n    \"torch._tensor\",\n    \"torch.amp.autocast_mode\",\n    \"torch.ao.nn\",\n    \"torch.autograd.function\",\n    \"torch.backends.cuda\",\n    \"torch.cuda.amp.autocast_mode\",\n    \"torch.distributions\",\n    \"torch.export._tree_utils\",\n    \"torch.export._unlift\",\n    \"torch.export._wrapper_utils\",\n    \"torch.fx._pytree\",\n    \"torch.fx._symbolic_trace\",\n    \"torch.fx.experimental.proxy_tensor\",\n    \"torch.fx.passes.shape_prop\",\n    \"torch.fx.traceback\",\n    \"torch.nn\",\n    \"torch.overrides\",\n    \"torch.random\",\n    \"torch.return_types\",\n    \"torch.sparse\",\n    \"torch.testing\",\n    \"torch.utils._content_store\",\n    \"torch.utils._contextlib\",\n    \"torch.utils._cxx_pytree\",\n    \"torch.utils._device\",\n    \"torch.utils._foreach_utils\",\n    \"torch.utils._python_dispatch\",\n    \"torch.utils._pytree\",\n    \"torch.utils.hooks\",\n]\nassert sorted(set(MOD_INLINELIST)) == MOD_INLINELIST\nMOD_INLINELIST = set(MOD_INLINELIST)\n\n\nif torch.distributed.is_available():\n    MOD_INLINELIST.add(\"torch.distributed\")\n    if not config.skip_fsdp_hooks:\n        MOD_INLINELIST.add(\"torch.distributed.fsdp._fully_shard\")\n\n\n# By default, all functions under these modules are skipped.\n# All the other knobs\n# (torch_name_rule_map, MOD_INLINELIST, LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST)\n# take precedence over this list; e.g. if a function is in\n# MOD_INLINELIST and MOD_SKIPLIST, then it will be inlined.\n# See \"A note on skip/inline rules\" for more details.\n#\n# The skip is NOT recursive. If a function f1 in a module in MOD_SKIPLIST\n# calls out to another function f2 in some other module, then Dynamo's\n# behavior (skip/inline) depends on what we've marked f2 as:\n# - if f2 is a function in a module in MOD_SKIPLIST, then we skip f2\n# - if f2 is a function in a module in MOD_INLINELIST, then we skip f2\n# - if f2 is a function in a module in LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST, then we inline f2\n# - if f2 is a function in a module not in any *_LIST, then we inline f2\nMOD_SKIPLIST = [\n    \"torch._VF\",\n    \"torch.__future__\",\n    \"torch.__init__\",\n    \"torch._awaits\",\n    \"torch._classes\",\n    \"torch._compile\",\n    \"torch._custom_op\",\n    \"torch._custom_ops\",\n    \"torch._decomp\",\n    \"torch._dispatch\",\n    \"torch._dynamo\",\n    \"torch._export\",\n    \"torch._functorch\",\n    \"torch._guards\",\n    \"torch._higher_order_ops.effects\",\n    \"torch._higher_order_ops.torchbind\",\n    \"torch._higher_order_ops.wrap\",\n    \"torch._inductor\",\n    \"torch._jit_internal\",\n    \"torch._lazy\",\n    \"torch._library\",\n    \"torch._linalg_utils\",\n    \"torch._lobpcg\",\n    \"torch._logging\",\n    \"torch._lowrank\",\n    \"torch._meta_registrations\",\n    \"torch._namedtensor_internals\",\n    \"torch._numpy\",\n    \"torch._ops\",\n    \"torch._prims\",\n    \"torch._prims_common\",\n    \"torch._python_dispatcher\",\n    \"torch._refs\",\n    \"torch._strobelight\",\n    \"torch._subclasses\",\n    \"torch._tensor\",\n    \"torch._tensor_str\",\n    \"torch._thread_safe_fork\",\n    \"torch._utils\",\n    \"torch._utils_internal\",\n    \"torch._vmap_internals\",\n    \"torch._weights_only_unpickler\",\n    \"torch.accelerator\",\n    \"torch.amp\",\n    \"torch.ao\",\n    \"torch.autograd\",\n    \"torch.backends\",\n    \"torch.compiler\",\n    \"torch.contrib\",\n    \"torch.cpu\",\n    \"torch.cuda\",\n    \"torch.distributed\",\n    \"torch.distributions\",\n    \"torch.export\",\n    \"torch.fb\",\n    \"torch.fft\",\n    \"torch.functional\",\n    \"torch.futures\",\n    \"torch.fx\",\n    \"torch.hub\",\n    \"torch.jit\",\n    \"torch.library\",\n    \"torch.linalg\",\n    \"torch.masked\",\n    \"torch.monitor\",\n    \"torch.mps\",\n    \"torch.mtia\",\n    \"torch.multiprocessing\",\n    \"torch.nested\",\n    \"torch.nn\",\n    \"torch.onnx\",\n    \"torch.overrides\",\n    \"torch.package\",\n    \"torch.profiler\",\n    \"torch.quantization\",\n    \"torch.quasirandom\",\n    \"torch.random\",\n    \"torch.serialization\",\n    \"torch.signal\",\n    \"torch.sparse\",\n    \"torch.special\",\n    \"torch.storage\",\n    \"torch.testing\",\n    \"torch.types\",\n    \"torch.utils\",\n    \"torch.xpu\",\n]\n\nassert sorted(set(MOD_SKIPLIST)) == MOD_SKIPLIST\nMOD_SKIPLIST = set(MOD_SKIPLIST)\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_legacy_mod_inlinelist() -> set[str]:\n    torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n    if torch_dir is None:\n        return set()\n    inlinelist = {\n        _as_posix_path(torch_dir + m[len(\"torch.\") :].replace(\".\", \"/\"))\n        for m in LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST\n    }\n    return inlinelist\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_mod_inlinelist() -> set[str]:\n    torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n    if torch_dir is None:\n        return set()\n    inlinelist = {\n        _as_posix_path(torch_dir + m[len(\"torch.\") :].replace(\".\", \"/\"))\n        for m in MOD_INLINELIST\n    }\n    return inlinelist\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef get_mod_skiplist() -> set[str]:\n    torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n    if torch_dir is None:\n        return set()\n    skiplist = {\n        _as_posix_path(torch_dir + m[len(\"torch.\") :].replace(\".\", \"/\"))\n        for m in MOD_SKIPLIST\n    }\n    return skiplist\n\n\n# skip some standard python builtin libs\nSKIP_DIRS = [\n    \"<frozen importlib\",\n    \"<frozen abc\",\n    \"<__array_function__ internals>\",\n    _as_posix_path(_config_module.__file__),\n    \"triton/backends\",\n]\nSKIP_DIRS.extend(map(_as_posix_path, filter(None, map(_module_dir, BUILTIN_SKIPLIST))))\n\nSKIP_DIRS_RE = re.compile(r\"match nothing^\")\n\n# Skip fbcode paths(including torch.package paths) containing\n# one of the following strings.\nFBCODE_SKIP_DIRS: set[str] = set()\n\nFBCODE_SKIP_DIRS_RE = re.compile(f\".*({'|'.join(map(re.escape, FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS))})\")\n\n# Remove this after fbcode is fully migrated to tracing through torchrec.\nFBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS = {\n    \"torchrec/distributed\",\n    \"torchrec/fb/distributed\",\n    \"caffe2/torch/fb/sparsenn/pooled_embeddings_modules.py\",\n}\n\nFBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS_RE = re.compile(\n    f\".*({'|'.join(re.escape(_as_posix_path(d)) for d in FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS)})\"\n)\n\n# TODO(yanboliang, anijain2305) - There are a few concerns that we should\n# resolve\n# 1) Audit if torchrec/distributed is even required in FBCODE_SKIPS_DIR\n# 2) To inline just one file but skip others in a directory, we could use\n# manual_torch_name_rule_map but this one is hard because FBCODE can add unusual\n# names like torch_package.\n# So, this is a stop gap solution till then.\nFBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS = {\n    \"torchrec/distributed/types.py\",\n}\nFBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS_RE = re.compile(\n    f\".*({'|'.join(re.escape(_as_posix_path(d)) for d in FBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS)})\"\n)\n\n# torch.optim is a special case,\n# we usually want to inline it, but the directory\n# structure does not match the module structure\n# and we want to skip the functions in optim/lr_scheduler.py\n# this has precedence over all other rules in check_file\nFORCE_SKIP_FILES = {f\"{_module_dir(torch)}optim/lr_scheduler.py\"}\n\n\ndef _recompile_re() -> None:\n    global SKIP_DIRS_RE\n    SKIP_DIRS_RE = re.compile(\n        rf\"^[^\\s<]*({'|'.join(re.escape(_as_posix_path(d)) for d in SKIP_DIRS)})\"\n    )\n\n\ndef add(import_name: str) -> None:\n    if isinstance(import_name, types.ModuleType):\n        return add(import_name.__name__)\n    assert isinstance(import_name, str)\n    from importlib.util import find_spec\n\n    module_spec = find_spec(import_name)\n    if not module_spec:\n        return\n    origin = module_spec.origin\n    if origin is None:\n        return\n    SKIP_DIRS.append(_strip_init_py(origin))\n    _recompile_re()\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass SkipResult:\n    skipped: bool\n    reason: Optional[str]\n\n\ndef check_file(filename: Optional[str], is_inlined_call: bool = False) -> SkipResult:\n    \"\"\"Should skip this file?\"\"\"\n    if filename is None:\n        return SkipResult(True, \"filename is None\")\n    filename = _as_posix_path(filename)\n    if filename in FORCE_SKIP_FILES:\n        return SkipResult(True, \"FORCE_SKIP_FILES\")\n\n    if any(filename.startswith(d) for d in get_legacy_mod_inlinelist()):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            \"LEGACY_MOD_INLINELIST\",\n        )\n    if is_inlined_call and is_torch_inline_allowed(filename):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            \"MOD_INLINELIST\",\n        )\n    if (\n        is_fbcode()\n        and FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS\n        and bool(FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n        and not bool(FBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(\n            True,\n            \"FBCODE_SKIP_DIRS\",\n        )\n\n    if (\n        is_fbcode()\n        and config.skip_torchrec\n        and FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS\n        and bool(FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n        and not bool(FBCODE_INLINE_FILES_IN_SKIPPED_DIRS_RE.match(filename))\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"FBCODE_SKIP_TORCHREC_DIRS\")\n\n    unittest_dir = _module_dir(unittest)\n    if (\n        unittest_dir is not None\n        and filename.startswith(unittest_dir)\n        and not torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"unittest\")\n\n    if bool(SKIP_DIRS_RE.match(filename)):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"SKIP_DIRS\")\n\n    if any(filename.startswith(d) for d in get_mod_skiplist()):\n        return SkipResult(True, \"MOD_SKIPLIST\")\n    return SkipResult(False, \"inlined by default\")\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass FunctionInfo:\n    py_obj: Optional[object]\n    name: Optional[str]\n    filename: str\n    code: Optional[types.CodeType]\n\n\n\"\"\"\nThis is the main entry point to determine whether an object (function) should be inlined or skipped.\nLet's illustrate the logic with an example:\n    @torch.compile\n    def f1(x, y):\n        ......\n        f2(x, y)\n        ......\n\n    def f2(x, y):\n        ......\n        f3(x, y)\n        ......\n\n    def f3(x, y):\n        ......\n\nThere are mainly three call sites of check/check_verbose:\n* The compile region entrance (like function f1), the corresponding code is located at eval_frame.py.\n* When tracing the recursively called functions (like function f2 and f3).\n    * Dynamo decides inline/skip every time it encounters a new recursively function call, and the call site\n      is in InliningInstructionTranslator.check_inlineable of symbolic_convert.py.\n    * If f2 is skipped by Dynamo, when evaluating the frame of f3, Dynamo need the inline/skip check again\n      and the call site is in catch_errors_wrapper.catch_errors of convert_frame.py.\n* For global variables and function arguments, Dynamo needs to decide if they are wrapped as SkipFunctionVariable in builder.py.\n\n`is_inlined_call` is used to indicate if the current function call is inlined (f2 is inlined call if it passes check)\nor not (f3 is not inlined call if f2 is skipped). Inside of the `check_verbose` function, there are more rules\nto be checked if this `is_inlined_call`.\nThe reason to have this flag is that if the upper level function call (e.g, f2) is skipped,\nwe don't want to inline the lower level function call (e.g, f3) by default.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef check_verbose(obj: Any, is_inlined_call: bool = False) -> SkipResult:\n    if isinstance(\n        obj,\n        (\n            UserFunctionVariable,\n            UserMethodVariable,\n            NestedUserFunctionVariable,\n            LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n            LocalGeneratorObjectVariable,\n        ),\n    ):\n        try:\n            py_obj = obj.get_function()\n        except NotImplementedError:\n            py_obj = None\n        fi = FunctionInfo(py_obj, obj.get_name(), obj.get_filename(), obj.get_code())\n    elif isinstance(obj, types.CodeType):\n        fi = FunctionInfo(None, obj.co_name, obj.co_filename, obj)\n    elif isinstance(obj, (types.FunctionType, types.MethodType)):\n        filename = getfile(obj)\n        assert filename is not None\n        fi = FunctionInfo(\n            obj,\n            obj.__name__,\n            filename,\n            obj.__code__,  # type: ignore[union-attr] # FIXME Add MethodType.__code__ to typeshed\n        )\n    else:\n        filename = getfile(obj)\n        assert filename is not None\n        fi = FunctionInfo(obj, None, filename, None)\n\n    # Consulte the central trace rules defined in torch._dynamo.trace_rules.\n    reasons: set[str] = set()\n    rule = lookup_inner(fi.py_obj, fi.name, fi.filename, is_inlined_call, reasons)\n    assert rule is not None\n    if issubclass(\n        rule,\n        (\n            UserFunctionVariable,\n            LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n            PolyfilledFunctionVariable,\n        ),\n    ):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            f\"inlined according trace_rules.lookup {reasons.pop()}\",\n        )\n    elif issubclass(rule, TorchInGraphFunctionVariable):\n        return SkipResult(\n            False,\n            f\"registered in torch_obj_rule {reasons.pop()}\",\n        )\n    else:\n        assert rule == SkipFunctionVariable, rule\n        return SkipResult(\n            True,\n            f\"skipped according trace_rules.lookup {reasons.pop()}\",\n        )\n\n\ndef check(obj: Any, is_inlined_call: bool = False) -> bool:\n    return check_verbose(obj, is_inlined_call).skipped\n\n\n# skip common third party libs\nfor _name in THIRDPARTY_SKIPLIST:\n    add(_name)\n\n_recompile_re()\n\n\ndef is_torch_inline_allowed(filename: str) -> bool:\n    return any(filename.startswith(d) for d in get_mod_inlinelist())\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef dynamo_dir() -> Optional[str]:\n    import torch._dynamo\n\n    return _module_dir(torch._dynamo)\n\n\ndef is_torch(filename: str) -> bool:\n    dynamo_path = dynamo_dir()\n    if dynamo_path is not None and filename.startswith(dynamo_path):\n        return False\n    torch_path = _module_dir(torch)\n    return torch_path is not None and filename.startswith(torch_path)\n\n\n\"\"\"\nMain entry point for looking up the trace rule (the Dynamo variable) for a given callable object.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef lookup_callable(obj: Callable[..., Any]) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    if not hashable(obj):\n        return None\n    # Custom allow/disallow in graph takes precedence over the general lookup.\n    if is_callable_disallowed(obj):\n        return SkipFunctionVariable\n    if is_callable_allowed(obj):\n        return TorchInGraphFunctionVariable\n    if is_polyfilled_callable(obj):\n        return PolyfilledFunctionVariable\n    if is_builtin_callable(obj):\n        return BuiltinVariable\n    return None\n\n\n\"\"\"\nMain entry point for looking up the trace rule (the Dynamo variable) for a given function object.\nE.g, the lookup result of `torch.sin` is `TorchInGraphFunctionVariable`.\n\"\"\"\n\n\ndef lookup(obj: Any) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    return lookup_inner(obj)\n\n\n# also takes config.dont_skip_tracing into account\ndef lookup_inner(\n    obj: Any,\n    name: Optional[str] = None,\n    filename: Optional[str] = None,\n    is_direct_call: bool = True,\n    reasons: Union[None, set[str]] = None,\n) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    result = _lookup_inner(\n        obj,\n        name=name,\n        filename=filename,\n        is_direct_call=is_direct_call,\n        reasons=reasons,\n    )\n    # There are still some modules we should absolutely NOT trace into - e.g. most of torch._dynamo,\n    # as this can result in really weird tracing behaviors.\n    # Note that if a torch._dynamo function is already not skipped (e.g. functions in external_utils.py),\n    # then this branch does not apply.\n    if config.dont_skip_tracing and result is SkipFunctionVariable:\n        if filename is None:\n            filename = getfile(obj)\n        assert filename is not None\n        filename = _as_posix_path(filename)\n        torch_dir = _module_dir(torch)\n        if torch_dir is not None:\n            dynamo_path = _as_posix_path(torch_dir) + \"_dynamo\"\n            if filename.startswith(dynamo_path) and not filename.endswith(\n                \"test_dont_skip_tracing_functions.py\"\n            ):\n                return SkipFunctionVariable\n        if reasons is not None:\n            reasons.add(\n                \"Attempted skip but we are ignoring skips due to torch._dynamo.config.dont_skip_tracing\"\n            )\n        return UserFunctionVariable\n    return result\n\n\ndef _lookup_inner(\n    obj: Any,\n    name: Optional[str] = None,\n    filename: Optional[str] = None,\n    is_direct_call: bool = True,\n    reasons: Optional[set[str]] = None,\n) -> Optional[type[VariableTracker]]:\n    # Step 1: lookup obj's tracing rule in `torch_name_rule_map`.\n    # The rules defined in `torch_name_rule_map` mainly includes two parts:\n    # - Manually defined rules for any functions.\n    # - The list of torch in graph functions.\n    try:\n        can_hash = hashable(obj)\n    except Exception:\n        can_hash = False\n    if not can_hash:\n        if reasons is not None:\n            reasons.add(\"obj is not hashable\")\n        return None\n    if obj is not None:\n        if is_aten_op_or_tensor_method(obj):\n            return TorchInGraphFunctionVariable\n        rule = get_torch_obj_rule_map().get(obj, None)\n        if rule is not None:\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"get_torch_obj_rule_map\")\n            return rule\n    elif name is not None and filename is not None and not is_direct_call:\n        if name.startswith(TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX):\n            rule = get_torch_obj_rule_map().get(\n                filename + \"#\" + TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX, None\n            )\n        else:\n            rule = get_torch_obj_rule_map().get(filename + \"#\" + name, None)\n        if rule is not None:\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"get_torch_obj_rule_map\")\n            return rule\n    elif name == \"<listcomp>\":\n        if reasons is not None:\n            reasons.add(\"inlining frame from list comprehension\")\n        return UserFunctionVariable\n\n    # Step 2: lookup obj's tracing rule by function name.\n    if is_direct_call:\n        if name == \"patched_init\":\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"func name is patched_init\")\n            return SkipFunctionVariable\n        elif name == \"__torch_function__\" or (\n            obj and getattr(obj, \"__name__\", None) == \"__torch_function__\"\n        ):\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\"func name is __torch_function__\")\n            return UserFunctionVariable\n\n    if not is_direct_call:\n        if name == \"__getattr__\":\n            # is_direct_call = False indicates that this is the top-level frame\n            # being traced (i.e., it is not inlined and not called from\n            # InliningInstructionTranslator).  Tracing __getattr__ at the top\n            # level is unlikely because we inline it for\n            # UserDefinedObjectVariable. This scenario occurs only for\n            # UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, where Dynamo directly calls\n            # __getattr__ during trace time, generating LOAD_ATTR bytecode\n            # without going through the underlying __getattr__ data structures.\n            # When this optimized bytecode is executed, Dynamo is triggered\n            # again on the __getattr__ call. Therefore, we skip Dynamo tracing\n            # in this case.\n            if reasons is not None:\n                reasons.add(\n                    \"Tracing __getattr__ as the top level frame, unsuitable for tracing.\"\n                )\n            return SkipFunctionVariable\n\n    # Step 3: lookup obj's tracing rule by filename.\n    if filename is None:\n        filename = getfile(obj)\n\n    skip_result = check_file(filename, is_direct_call)\n    if reasons is not None and skip_result.reason is not None:\n        reasons.add(skip_result.reason)\n    if skip_result.skipped:\n        return SkipFunctionVariable\n    else:\n        return UserFunctionVariable\n\n\ndef clear_lru_cache() -> None:\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_torch_obj_rule_map.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_tensor_method.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_legacy_mod_inlinelist.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.get_mod_inlinelist.cache_clear()\n    torch._dynamo.trace_rules.dynamo_dir.cache_clear()\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "         \"torch._add_batch_dim\",",
        "         \"torch._add_relu_\",",
        "         \"torch._add_relu\",",
        "+        \"torch._addmm_activation\",",
        "         \"torch._aminmax\",",
        "         \"torch._amp_foreach_non_finite_check_and_unscale_\",",
        "         \"torch._amp_update_scale_\","
      ],
      "newLines": 7,

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/overrides.py",
  "newString": "        Tensor._is_zerotensor,\n        Tensor._is_all_true,\n        Tensor._is_any_true,\n        Tensor._addmm_activation,\n        Tensor.to_padded_tensor,",
  "oldString": "        Tensor._is_zerotensor,\n        Tensor._is_all_true,\n        Tensor._is_any_true,\n        Tensor.to_padded_tensor,",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nPython implementation of ``__torch_function__``\n\nWhile most of the torch API and handling for ``__torch_function__`` happens\nat the C++ level, some of the torch API is written in Python so we need\npython-level handling for ``__torch_function__`` overrides as well. The main\ndeveloper-facing functionality in this file are handle_torch_function and\nhas_torch_function. See torch/functional.py and test/test_overrides.py\nfor usage examples.\n\nNote\n----\nheavily inspired by NumPy's ``__array_function__`` (see:\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/24015 and\nhttps://www.numpy.org/neps/nep-0018-array-function-protocol.html\n)\n\nIf changing this file in a way that can affect ``__torch_function__`` overhead,\nplease report the benchmarks in ``benchmarks/overrides_benchmark``. See the\ninstructions in the ``README.md`` in that directory.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport __future__  # noqa: F404\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport functools\nimport sys\nimport types\nimport warnings\nfrom collections.abc import Callable, Iterable\nfrom functools import wraps\nfrom typing import Any, TypeVar\nfrom typing_extensions import ParamSpec\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch._C import (\n    _add_docstr,\n    _get_function_stack_at,\n    _has_torch_function,\n    _has_torch_function_unary,\n    _has_torch_function_variadic,\n    _is_torch_function_mode_enabled,\n    _len_torch_function_stack,\n    _pop_torch_function_stack,\n    _push_on_torch_function_stack,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"get_ignored_functions\",\n    \"get_overridable_functions\",\n    \"get_testing_overrides\",\n    \"handle_torch_function\",\n    \"has_torch_function\",\n    \"resolve_name\",\n    \"is_tensor_like\",\n    \"is_tensor_method_or_property\",\n    \"wrap_torch_function\",\n    \"enable_reentrant_dispatch\",\n]\n\n_P = ParamSpec(\"_P\")\n_R = TypeVar(\"_R\")\n\n\ndef _disable_user_warnings(\n    func: Callable[_P, _R],\n    regex: str = \".*is deprecated, please use.*\",\n    module: str = \"torch\",\n) -> Callable[_P, _R]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Decorator that temporarily disables ``UserWarning``s for the given ``module`` if the warning message matches the\n    given ``regex`` pattern.\n\n    Arguments\n    ---------\n    func : function\n        Function to disable the warnings for.\n    regex : str\n        A regex pattern compilable by ``re.compile``. This is used to match the ``UserWarning`` message.\n    module : str\n        The python module to which the filtering should be restricted.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    function\n        The wrapped function.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @wraps(func)\n    def wrapper(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _R:\n        with warnings.catch_warnings():\n            warnings.filterwarnings(\n                \"ignore\", category=UserWarning, message=regex, module=module\n            )\n            return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    return wrapper\n\n\n@functools.cache\n@_disable_user_warnings\ndef get_ignored_functions() -> set[Callable]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return public functions that cannot be overridden by ``__torch_function__``.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    set[Callable]\n        A tuple of functions that are publicly available in the torch API but cannot\n        be overridden with ``__torch_function__``. Mostly this is because none of the\n        arguments of these functions are tensors or tensor-likes.\n\n    Examples\n    --------\n    >>> torch.Tensor.as_subclass in torch.overrides.get_ignored_functions()\n    True\n    >>> torch.add in torch.overrides.get_ignored_functions()\n    False\n    \"\"\"\n    Tensor = torch.Tensor\n    functions = {\n        torch.typename,\n        torch.is_tensor,\n        torch.is_storage,\n        torch.set_default_tensor_type,\n        torch.set_default_device,\n        torch.get_default_device,\n        torch.set_rng_state,\n        torch.get_rng_state,\n        torch.manual_seed,\n        torch.initial_seed,\n        torch.seed,\n        torch.save,\n        torch.load,\n        torch.set_printoptions,\n        torch.fork,\n        torch.get_default_dtype,\n        torch.get_num_interop_threads,\n        torch.get_num_threads,\n        torch.init_num_threads,\n        torch.import_ir_module,\n        torch.import_ir_module_from_buffer,\n        torch.is_anomaly_enabled,\n        torch.is_anomaly_check_nan_enabled,\n        torch.is_grad_enabled,\n        torch.merge_type_from_type_comment,\n        torch.parse_ir,\n        torch.parse_schema,\n        torch.parse_type_comment,\n        torch.set_anomaly_enabled,\n        torch.set_flush_denormal,\n        torch.set_num_interop_threads,\n        torch.set_num_threads,\n        torch.wait,\n        torch.as_tensor,\n        torch.from_numpy,\n        torch.tensor,\n        torch.default_generator,\n        torch.has_cuda,\n        torch.has_cudnn,\n        torch.has_lapack,\n        torch.device,\n        torch.dtype,\n        torch.finfo,\n        torch.has_mkl,\n        torch.has_mps,\n        torch.has_mkldnn,\n        torch.has_openmp,\n        torch.iinfo,\n        torch.memory_format,\n        torch.qscheme,\n        torch.set_grad_enabled,\n        torch.no_grad,\n        torch.enable_grad,\n        torch.inference_mode,\n        torch.is_inference_mode_enabled,\n        torch.layout,\n        torch.align_tensors,\n        torch.arange,\n        torch.as_strided,\n        torch.bartlett_window,\n        torch.blackman_window,\n        torch.broadcast_shapes,\n        torch.can_cast,\n        torch.compile,\n        torch.cudnn_affine_grid_generator,\n        torch.cudnn_batch_norm,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution_transpose,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution_relu,\n        torch.cudnn_convolution_add_relu,\n        torch.cudnn_grid_sampler,\n        torch.cudnn_is_acceptable,\n        torch.empty,\n        torch.empty_permuted,\n        torch.empty_strided,\n        torch.empty_quantized,\n        torch.export.export,\n        torch.export.load,\n        torch.export.register_dataclass,\n        torch.export.save,\n        torch.eye,\n        torch.fft.fftfreq,\n        torch.fft.rfftfreq,\n        torch.from_file,\n        torch.full,\n        torch.fill,\n        torch.hamming_window,\n        torch.hann_window,\n        torch.kaiser_window,\n        torch.linspace,\n        torch.logspace,\n        torch.mkldnn_adaptive_avg_pool2d,\n        torch.mkldnn_convolution,\n        torch.mkldnn_max_pool2d,\n        torch.mkldnn_max_pool3d,\n        torch.mkldnn_linear_backward_weights,\n        torch.mkldnn_rnn_layer,\n        torch.normal,\n        torch.ones,\n        torch.promote_types,\n        torch.rand,\n        torch.rand_like,\n        torch.randn,\n        torch.randn_like,\n        torch.randint,\n        torch.randint_like,\n        torch.randperm,\n        torch.range,\n        torch.result_type,\n        torch.scalar_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_coo_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_compressed_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_csr_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_csc_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_bsr_tensor,\n        torch.sparse_bsc_tensor,\n        torch.sym_constrain_range,\n        torch.sym_constrain_range_for_size,\n        torch.sym_fresh_size,\n        torch.tril_indices,\n        torch.triu_indices,\n        torch.vander,\n        torch.zeros,\n        torch._jit_internal.boolean_dispatch,\n        torch.nn.functional.assert_int_or_pair,\n        torch.nn.functional.upsample,\n        torch.nn.functional.upsample_bilinear,\n        torch.nn.functional.upsample_nearest,\n        torch.nn.functional.has_torch_function,\n        torch.nn.functional.has_torch_function_unary,\n        torch.nn.functional.has_torch_function_variadic,\n        torch.nn.functional.handle_torch_function,\n        torch.nn.functional.grouped_mm,\n        torch.nn.functional.scaled_grouped_mm,\n        torch.nn.functional.scaled_mm,\n        torch.nn.functional.sigmoid,\n        torch.nn.functional.hardsigmoid,\n        torch.nn.functional.tanh,\n        torch.nn.functional._canonical_mask,\n        torch.nn.functional._none_or_dtype,\n        # Doesn't actually take or return tensor arguments\n        torch.nn.init.calculate_gain,\n        # These are deprecated; don't test them\n        torch.nn.init.uniform,\n        torch.nn.init.normal,\n        torch.nn.init.constant,\n        torch.nn.init.eye,\n        torch.nn.init.dirac,\n        torch.nn.init.xavier_uniform,\n
